title: No no
review: Don't understand why everyone is praising this movie. It felt like something made for 8-15-year-olds. I was literally waiting for it to end. The story was basic, the humor was childish, and overall it felt like a stretched-out SNL skit. Definitely not made for adults.don't understand why everyone is praising this movie. It felt like something made for 8-15-year-olds. I was literally waiting for it to end. The story was basic, the humor was childish, and overall it felt like a stretched-out SNL skit. Definitely not made for adults.

title: Overrated
review:
What a disappointment the movie was! A pale mix of Interstellar and The Martian, but not grounded in science or facts-more like a children's film. What has happened to today's movie audience? Don't people have higher expectations for filmmaking anymore? The music in the film made it even worse. I almost walked out halfway through, but I thought it had to get better considering the reviews...
These are 2 hours and 37 minutes of my life that I will never get back.

title: Boring and not fun at all, or how to waste millions uselessly
review: Awful, horrible. I wish it were a B-movie, but it's not even that. Unoriginal, absurd and ridiculous acting and dialogue. Full of terrible jokes. My recommendation: don't watch this movie.The only redeeming features are the acceptable set design and costumes. The special effects are mediocre.I regret that Edward Gosling accepted this challenge. He's undoubtedly an excellent actor, but he made a whimsical film of dubious artistic merit.

title: Project Hail Mary? You'll be praying for it to end! DO NOT BELIEVE THE HYPE!!!
Not the Sci-Fi I Was Expecting
review: I watched Project Hail Mary at the cinema last night, and honestly, it was not what I expected at all. I went in thinking it would be a serious, intense science fiction movie-something along the lines of Interstellar. Instead, it turned out to be much more lighthearted and comedy-focused than I anticipated.While the movie does have a science fiction setting, the tone felt too playful and humorous for my taste. It didn't really give me that deep, thought-provoking feeling that I usually look for in sci-fi films. The constant jokes and less serious approach made it harder to take the story seriously.If you're someone who enjoys funny or more relaxed sci-fi, you might actually like it. But for viewers who prefer realistic, serious science fiction with a strong emotional or philosophical impact, this probably won't meet expectations.Personally, I wouldn't recommend it to anyone looking for a "real" sci-fi experience.

title: The world really has gone mad...
review: This movie currently has a rating of 8.4? How is this possible? Insultingly cringe "humour" and a "plot" aimed squarely at people with an attention span of about 5 seconds per scene.I thought "Gravity" was the worst space film ever made, but this made Sandra Bullock's "eeny meeny miney..." drivel look sophisticated.Being cinematically impressive doesn't rescue intellectual drivel. I reckon if I was 10 years old I'd still feel patronised and insulted.Absolute dross. Made for the Marvel Generation. Avoid at ALL COSTS.

title: Stupid movie with stupid plot coated with high school level science
review: And with zero scientific thought process. It's an insult to the intelligence of every serious scientist. I can't think about a worse sci-fi I have ever encountered in my 50 years of intensive sci-fi reading and viewing. I am so disappointed with Andy Weir and don't understand how can a writer degrade himself so exponentially.

title: Not creative
review: The film seems targeted at a younger audience and lacks meaningful content. It leans heavily on familiar Bollywood clichés, which may come across as cringeworthy at times. Best enjoyed casually-don't expect anything groundbreaking.Hail Mary" is a deeply disappointing film that struggles on almost every front. From the very beginning, it becomes clear that the movie lacks a clear sense of direction, relying heavily on clichés and predictable storytelling. The plot feels extremely shallow, with little to no effort made to build tension or create meaningful engagement. Instead of offering something fresh or thought-provoking, it simply recycles overused ideas in a way that feels uninspired and outdated.One of the biggest issues with the film is its weak writing. The screenplay is filled with unrealistic situations and forced moments that fail to connect emotionally. Dialogues often come across as artificial and exaggerated, making it difficult to take the characters seriously. Speaking of characters, they are poorly developed and lack depth, which makes it hard for the audience to care about their journeys or outcomes. Their actions frequently feel illogical, further breaking immersion.The film also suffers from tonal inconsistency. At times it tries to be serious, while at others it leans into over-the-top, almost cartoonish elements, creating a confusing viewing experience. Instead of blending genres effectively, it ends up feeling like a disjointed mix that never quite works. The direction does little to elevate the material, and many scenes feel unnecessarily stretched or repetitive.Another major drawback is the over-reliance on typical, formulaic elements that have been seen countless times before. There is no real sense of creativity or innovation, and the movie fails to stand out in any meaningful way. What could have been an engaging story ends up feeling like a missed opportunity.Overall, "Hail Mary" is a film that offers very little in terms of substance or originality. It feels like a forgettable attempt at entertainment that doesn't justify the time invested in watching it. Unless you have an extremely high tolerance for clichéd and underwhelming cinema, this is one movie that can easily be skipped.

title: Shockingly Poor Film
review: Went into this with a very open mind impressed with the 8.4* on IMDB, left very concerned of a mass possession. There is nothing redeeming about this film unless you are under seven years old or a Ryan Gosling super fan.Filled with flat jokes and a nonsensical storyline, the entire film has no grounding in reality. Ok sure you might say, what were you expecting when Ryan goes into space to make friends with a rock monster? Well, at least a semblance of scientists acting like the world was ending when it was and half believable explanation for the way all the laws of physics were bastardised. Might have been explained in the second half, I left after an hour.

title: Don' waste your time!
review: Boring and depressing. Almost the whole movie you're watching one actor playing. Both the movie and the novel simply confirm that human creativity is degrading.If you want to watch a really great movie or read a great science fiction novel, take Contact (1997 movie) based on the novel Contact (1985) by Carl Sagan.

title: Boring, illogical, children's movie
review: It felt like something made for 8-15-year-Olds. Contrary to massive PR lying campaign this is not intelligent nor science nor good. I went in thinking it would be a serious, intense science fiction movie-something along the lines of Interstellar. This movie being rated so high seems suspicious. AI reviews? Backend industry prerogative? Dumb alien really? Same job being done by a school teacher is genius but same job by alien and he's so dumb?

title: Don't recommended above age of 10
review: This movie is a bad, very bad, joke unless you're ten years old or younger. The plot is ridiculous. From someone who wrote The Martian, I had much higher expectations. At least there was some science in the previous movie based on the book of the same author. This one is more suitable for the young audience from the kindergarten.

title: I Saved the Universe with my Dog
review:
Huge DisAppointment for Sci-Fi lovers...
It's a Child Tale with Duck Tape "Science"...
Should be Restricted ABOVE 10 year age!!!The "Acting" was also either horribly Flat or OverDone, but a Good Sci-Fi could work without that too.PS: I cannot get back my Wasted TIME, But you still can Save Yours =)

title: This movie confirms Hollywood is dead
review: This movie is an embarrassment for Hollywood. One could argue its a charming kids movie and not to take it seriously but there no chance a kid could sit still through almost 3 hours of this absolute bullsh$t. I am totally convinced the ratings are rigged. Everything about this movie is stupid: the stupid jokes, the stupid rock alien, the stupid glass body suits, the stupid translator. Please don't - just don't. You've been warned.

title: Scientifically offensive kid's movie in hell
review: Contrary to massive PR lying campaign this is not intelligent nor science nor good. It has tender very moving movie moments but it's in a dystopian soup of offensively unintelligent scifi while claiming to be intelligent. It's toy story in hell with cartoon science. And it's 2½ hours long. Don't be sheep and give them your money. It's not visually impressive either at all, that's another shameless PR lie.I wouldn't rate this 1 if they didn't lie so much and so many sheep weren't praising it, paid or otherwise.The premise of the movie is that multiple stars are suddenly being eaten by bacteria at the same time at a pace millions of times faster than the stars' own thermonuclear explosions. Never mind that bacteria don't have space propulsion nor would survive thousands of degrees of the sun. The creature is also implausible as are many other story elements. It's not clever, it's not intelligent, only the scientifically illiterate are fooled that it is. It's like Lost. Or interstellar for that matter. Junk science by liars for sheep. But ignoring all that it is a drawn out tender cartoon buddy movie. I'd give it 4, maybe 5 if they promoted it as such without all the effing lying.

title: Dull, unimaginative and emptish
review: It's fascinating how they promote a poor quality movie as something spectacular. I understand that in some countries government finances film industry, which is often run by uncreative kids of politicians or rich people, resulting in trashy unartistic movies. I wonder what caused such a decline in quality of the US films.

title: Waste of time
review: I don't know what this movie should be. It was neither science fiction nor anything. There is no story or plot behind. Nobody laughed, bit also not many left during the movie. Just bad and way too long. Acting was okay. Even the music was bad. The music would have been better for a spring flower documentary, except some of the songs.

title: Was this satire?
review: After reading the many positive reviews and plot, I was convinced to buy a ticket and go watch it. Boy I was wrong... The beginning of the movie is interesting and compelling, with lots of plot holes but still a cool scientific premise. And then it just takes a turn into this dumb and ridiculous story that is so wacky and not what the beginning portion built it out to be. Such a waste of 2.30hrs. Every few minutes I thought to myself "really... come on..." was tempted to walk out a few times. Cinematically it was stunning, but that's it.

title: boring
review: Boring movie with no story or plot . Waste of time to see. Horrible acting like something you see high schoolers make. Don't waste money or time watching movie. Movie has no soul nothing to keep you in your seat.actors had no acting ability.the movie has to many flashbacks. Complete lack of story as written.

title: Overlong Story
review: Possibly the worst movie I ever saw in a theatre. The story in the movie was really poor. None of the plot twists gave any real sense of what was going to happen next, so everything just felt confusing rather than exciting. On top of that, many of the scenes were unnecessarily long-honestly, about three-fifths of them could have been cut without losing anything important.

title: Worst movie I've ever seen
review: I've seen some pretty bad movies. I saw this in the theater with some friends and My child. I couldn't wait till it was over. I actually ended up leaving the theater to go to the bathroom and not walking back in. There are way too many things that don't make sense and Farr too many things don't add. For example, how does an idiot get to go into space with a group of rocket scientists. How does he get to communicate with life forms suddenly without much effort at all? There is zero suspension of disbelief. Ryan Gosling has been way too many movies now. I wish you would take a break because this type of role did not need to be played by him. Couldn't stop thinking about other idiot roles he's played like Ken in the movie Barbie. This movie was totally made for the Bros and the manosphere who wish they were him. I've actually never walked out of a movie before, so at least have accomplished that now.

title: Interstellar from temu
review: It's honestly hard to know where to begin with this film, because almost nothing about it works. The hype surrounding it feels completely undeserved. At its core, the movie comes across as a patchwork of ideas borrowed from better films-echoes of Interstellar, Moon, The Martian, and even 2001: A Space Odyssey are impossible to ignore-but without the originality or depth that made those films memorable.The tone is one of its biggest failures. It never fully commits to being a drama, yet its attempts at humor fall flat. The result is an awkward middle ground where nothing lands the way it should. The dialogue doesn't help either-it often feels unnatural or outright absurd, making it difficult to take any moment seriously.Then there's the story itself, which is riddled with glaring logical gaps. Suspension of disbelief can only go so far, and here it's constantly pushed past its limits. Character development is practically nonexistent; arcs feel incomplete or entirely absent, leaving no emotional anchor for the audience. It's hard to engage with a film when you don't care about anyone in it.As for Ryan Gosling, his performance raises questions-not necessarily about his talent, but about the project itself. It's difficult to see what drew him to this role. Even a stronger script might have allowed him to shine, but here, the material simply isn't there. No actor, no matter how skilled, could elevate something this fundamentally flawed-and one could argue that truly top-tier performers might have passed on it altogether.Visually, the film has been praised, but even that feels overstated. When compared to the likes of Dune, Blade Runner 2049, or Gravity, it falls short. And placing it anywhere near 2001: A Space Odyssey-a film that remains groundbreaking decades later-is almost absurd. In fact, many modern TV productions surpass it in terms of visual execution.In the end, this is a film that promises far more than it delivers. It lacks originality, coherence, and emotional impact. At over two and a half hours, it feels like a significant investment of time with very little payoff.Sincerely-this is one you can skip.

title: Let me save you some time, money and outrage. THIS IS A KIDS MOVIE
review: This is EXACTLY what pixar will do if they make an apocaliptic movie. No more, no less. They send a goofy charchter to save the world with his goofiness and he befriends an alien.About an hour into the movie you realized that you walked into a trap, and you will have to make a choice either to leave the theater or stand the pain to the end. I did it, so you dont have to.

title: Fell far far short of The Martian
review: Where ridley scott managed to pull an almost 1:1 copy of the hard sci-fi story The Martian when translating it from book to screen, these directors took extensive liberties with the source material and still abjectly failed to make a decent product.Even with a runtime of more than 2.5 hours, it still felt like they chopped out 50% of the book.And in this case, that means 75% of the SCIENCE is GONEBut they still find the minutes to have Ryan Gosling pull several awkward dance moves, thank god!Speaking of Gosling, they turned what was a groan-inducing dad of a teacher with a big wrinkly brain and an even bigger heart into a bumbling, hapless, cowardly fool.Gone is the careful dance of first-contact between two intelligent species, instead we get to see gosling cower inside his ship in a panic, and then accidentally trip and flop around on his tether in zero-g. "LOL!", right??? And don't forget the dance moves! Haha look at the rock copying him, so cute! So jiggly and funny! /sWhat the book had in hard science, sober internal drama mixed with dad-brain humor, and moments of intense descriptive writing, (the mission above Adrian foremost in my mind), the movie has in...cludge slapstick, an overworked orchestra, and red-herring non-sequiters of "pretty" cgi sparkles sprinkled around at the directors' fiat.Of the 2.5h: 45minutes was nauseating, 45 minutes was blatant exposition that DIDN'T cut Every LAST Corner, (elevating it from nauseating), 45 minutes was /yeah ok mediocre/, and 15 minutes were Actually Good.Huge disappointment.

title: Interstellar Expectations, Teletubby Reality
review: I honestly cannot fathom the high ratings this film has received. Based on the trailer, I expected a grand sci-fi epic that would leave Interstellar or 2001: A Space Odyssey in the cosmic dust. The reality? It's a children's comedy best suited for the 12-to-18-year-old demographic.This is a story about the single greatest threat to human existence, yet it's directed and performed like a sitcom. The tonal mismatch is so jarring that I would have walked out after 30 minutes if I hadn't already dropped $45 on tickets. What's even more depressing is that thousands of (presumably) adults have rated it so highly-it seems our collective desperation for "light relief" from our current world leaders actions, has lowered the bar for cinema.If you're looking for a thought-provoking, intellectual journey that sparks deep reflection, stay far away. But if you want a bit of light, "hummus-style" relief from life's stresses, by all means, go and enjoy the cosmic joke.

title: They cut too much of a good book. Disappointed results.
review: I really enjoyed reading the book, but the movie has a lack of plots, important dialogues, and essential moments. Probably it could be a better movie for someone who didn't red the book, but for those who read the book, it is a shame. However, I was excited to see the recreations of key several scenes in the big screen.

title: Brilliant book turned into unwatchable film
review: Why? Why Andy? Why did you allow to turn your brilliant story into this dumb film, where the alleged scientist behaves like Stiffler from American Pie? Where dialogues are so cheesy and "funny"? Where the weight of the impending doom is totally lost because of the forced bro-like vibe of the characters? Why "Deadpool" when it had potential to be "Interstellar"? Such a waste...

title: Ugh.
review: I've listened to the book several times on audiobook and it's one of my favorites. The original story is far fetched but full of wonder, tension, science, and emotion. The original is funny without trying too hard.I wanted to love this movie but it's terrible! It felt like watching a really long children's movie, but it's too slow for children to enjoy it. It's packed full of quippy modern jokes so that we don't have to think or feel (they don't succeed in making you laugh either). The music that does not match the action. The alien is dumb and childlike but somehow made it across the stars. There is a really long karaoke scene for some reason. The science, technology, and plot do not connect and flow together... We spend way too much time on things that don't matter, and skip over things that do.Some of the visuals were cool. But I hope to erase this movie from my memory.

title: Absolutely overhyped
review: It's strange to me that in reviews people rank this film on par with Interstellar or right after it, that's insane. There's practically an abyss between them. I strongly disliked both the plot and the acting, the story didn't move me at all, and I genuinely can't understand the hype around it. I'm giving it two stars for the visual effects, that's the only thing this film has going for it.

title: Surreally boring
review: You cannot imagine how meaningless this film is. Even though the main character is a "scientist", there is no trace of anything scientific in the film. Not a single idea in the film is grounded in any kind of reasoned concept. Nothing exists in this film except a simple childish story and a pretentious playlist.

title: Culture in decline - written for a 4 year old
review:
So superficial. I har to admit I skipped midway
Lame jokes constantly. ET phone home.Annoying and just a plane let down.Just irrelevant and boring. Even though I love sci fi - visuals not specifically spectacular- sounds design nothing special. Not worth the watch for adults. May a good movie for teenager or likeminded.

title: Confidently Dumb, Painfully Predictable
review: I love sci fi and wanted to like Project Hail Mary. Instead, it's two hours of hand-holding wrapped in faux science and emotional manipulation.The biggest problem is the science. This movie pretends to be grounded while relying on concepts that are pure fantasy, then has the audacity to explain them like a classroom lecture. It makes Star Trek look restrained, because at least Star Trek doesn't pretend its magic is real. Here, everything is presented with a straight face, as if saying it confidently enough makes it plausible. It doesn't.Then there's the central relationship. It's the most obvious, engineered emotional arc imaginable - no tension, no mistrust, no competing interests. Just instant friendship and predictable beats designed to squeeze out "awww" reactions. There's nothing alien about it. It's a buddy movie with a gimmick.The protagonist isn't much better. He exists mainly to narrate, explain, and move the plot forward. There's no complexity, no edge, and no real stakes beyond solving the next conveniently structured problem.And that's the pattern of the entire film: problem -> explanation -> solution. Rinse & repeat. No ambiguity, no risk, no real consequences. Just a clean, polished loop built for maximum accessibility and minimum thought.This isn't hard sci-fi. It's science-flavored comfort food pretending to be something smarter. They should have just gone full stupid and made Rocky a teddy bear.If you want something safe, predictable, and relentlessly optimistic, you'll probably enjoy it. If you're looking for anything intellectually or emotionally challenging, there's nothing here.

title: Made for 10 year olds
review: If you are 10 years old, I think you will really like this movie. We walked out in the first hour...I can't imagine staying for the whole film. I would really like a refund. They spent so much money for nothing. They try to be funny all the time. There's no serious break. It's like going to a standup routine in space but it's not even funny.

title: Missed something
review: First, I was not aware this was developed from a novel. I can only assume the novel was much better. The movie just wasn't that good. I thought it was too long and very choppy. It was a good idea poorly executed. There were enjoyable parts but there were other parts I wished for a remote so I could fast forward. Most of the earth based sections were over long and lacked any real chemistry. I would have to give this a hard pass. Can't understand the high ratings. The movie industry is really hurting. At least it wasn't a sequel or remake.

title: Booooooring...where are these good reviews coming from?
review: This movie wasn't just a waste of money, it felt like I paid to be mildly insulted. It's the kind of film that mistakes confusion for depth and awkwardness for artistry. The story is so hollow you could echo inside it, yet it struts around like it just solved philosophy.And the visuals? I've seen more compelling imagery from a dusty Winamp mp3 player visualizer, a Windows 98 screensaver, or a lava lamp that hasn't been plugged in since 2003.Honestly, this feels less like a movie and more like an influencer hype experiment flooded by bots on Instagram to see how many people will pretend they "get it." Don't fall for it, you deserve better.

title: If you read the book... don't watch.
Too Hollywood
review: Too freaking American, same Hollywood jokes, same Hollywood scripts, even the scenes are taken from Interstellar. People are tired of watching the same thing over and over and hearing the same jokes over and over again. Underestimating the intelligence of the average viewer. Never watching Hollywood American movies again.

title: Slow. Slow. Did I mention slow?
review: It was 2:36 min long. It felt 3.5 hrs long. The last 30 mins last forever. Sooo much musical manipulation. It's an Amazon movie. This needed a real studio behind to make cuts. Everyone wants to let directors do what they like and their "vision". But sometimes the vision is aimless and needs to be reigned in. This was at least 30 min too long. Gosling was great. Cinematography was great. Story was so slow and a microwaved version of the Martian and Interstellar mixed together.

title: So uninteresting
review: I give it two stars for the presents of Sandra Huller. She plays a character worth knowing. But the movie tried to be a mix of E. T. and 2001, and it just failed. Dull dialogue and no interesting direction. And over 2 and a half hours of it. The discovery of the alien should have been a stunner, but it just fell flat. It did have a Kris Kristofferson song, and another good song from Johannesburg, South Africa that was a hit awhile back. But both were at the beginning and then the movie just became a bore-bomb.

title: Hail Mary flop
review: Ryan gosling was the wrong man for the job on this one. The acting was laughable watching him play stupid for an entire movie while talking to a rock alien and it was a hour too long atleast. Emotionally detached at every moment, long drawn out and just boring as well. Ryan doesn't do well with green screen either.

title: A combination of the worst parts of Armageddon and E.T.
review: Truly, this is a dreadful film. For me it was like a cross between Armageddon, that awful movie which sent to ageing miners into space to save the world, and E. T. - except that it had only the worst parts of each film.The film is formulaic and tedious. Gosling is utterly without charisma, the alien is even more twee than E. T. and the whole film tries to set up one tear-jerking scenario after another ... endlessly ... and it doesn't work because you just don't care.I found that for the most part the gags fell flat. I mean, there were a few that made me chuckle, but they were few and far between.After the first 45 minutes I was begging for it to end, but oh no, they just kept on ... and on ... and on.If I had not gone with friends, I would have walked out of the cinema, it was that bad.In fairness, my friends were evenly divided between those who thought it was ok, and those who did not.

title: Silly
review: I like sci-fi and I am a big fan of Ryan Gosling but I would have changed the channel after the first 10 minutesif if it were playing on TV. Great special effects - for a generation of Idiocracy raised on video games. The plot is silly and all the interstellar adventure noisy and over the top. A waste of time and money for me, and a desperte shot at Oscars for Ryan Gosling,

title: Looking at my watch
review: This movie tries to be ET, Space Odyssey and prosbly half a dozen other sci fi space films. It is 90 minutes too long (it is almost 3 hours) and simply patched together story lines from well known films. I haven't looked at my watch once while watching a movie in years. I couldn't stop checking my watch during this film. Save your money and your time.

title: A failed sci-fi move
review: The film is a failed sci-fi project that betrays the genre's essence by replacing technical tension and rigor with cheap, absurd sentimentalism. For a viewer seeking the professionalism of films like Interstellar or Gravity, it is exasperating to watch a protagonist like Ryan Gosling's, who lacks any composure and breaks down emotionally every ten minutes without a solid narrative reason. The plot structure only worsens the experience, using flashbacks that feel like pure filler and attempting to force an emotional connection through funerals and mourning for unknown characters. This only succeeds in breaking the survival rhythm and turning what should have been an epic mission into a manipulative, childish drama that fails to respect the intelligence of those expecting true science fiction.

title: A Weak Film with No Clear Story
review: The film lacks a clear and coherent storyline, making it difficult for the audience to stay engaged or understand the overall direction of the plot. Events often feel disjointed, as if they are happening without a meaningful connection, which weakens the narrative structure. In addition, there are several scientific inaccuracies throughout the film that significantly affect its credibility. These errors may not be noticeable to all viewers, but for those with basic scientific knowledge, they can be distracting and reduce the film's realism.Another major drawback is the extremely limited number of characters in the film, as it features only two characters throughout the entire story. This lack of variety makes the film feel empty and less dynamic, reducing opportunities for meaningful interactions, conflict, and character development. As a result, the narrative feels restricted and less engaging.Furthermore, the character development itself is weak. The two characters are not given enough depth or background, making it hard for the audience to relate to them or care about their outcomes. Their motivations are often unclear, and their actions sometimes feel unrealistic or inconsistent, which further disconnects the viewer from the story.The pacing of the film is also problematic. Some scenes are unnecessarily long and slow, while others feel rushed and underdeveloped. This imbalance disrupts the viewing experience and prevents the film from building proper tension or emotional impact. Additionally, the dialogue often feels unnatural and forced, lacking authenticity and emotional depth.The visual effects, while ambitious, are not always convincing and sometimes appear poorly executed. Moreover, the film relies heavily on clichés and predictable tropes, offering little originality. Overall, these issues result in a film that lacks depth, credibility, and a satisfying cinematic experience.

title: Very disappointing
review: Almost as bad as Moonfall. It was very disjointed and hard to follow. Even the special effects were poor. It's almost like they had an idea, saw it wasn't working and decided to add some humor. That didn't work either. This movie is no Interstellar. Here's the analogy: Interstellar was MIT and this movie was grade school. The SFX in 2001 were better, and that was made almost 60 years ago!

title: Falls flat, poor writing
review: To me, it felt like they were trying to make a serious space movie, but ended making a very silly and superficial one. I could feel the moments that they were trying to make emotional/poignant, but there wasn't sufficient build up to make it work. I have never felt less emotionally connected to a character. The writing was so awkwardly paced that every would-be emotional moment fell flat. I wasn't invested in the character at all, because his growth felt haphazard and unearned. I didn't buy Ryan Gosling as a microbiologist. The movie felt like it had been written with zero input from any actual scientists. I could barely get past the logical absurdities of the plotline. I walked away unsure if this was actually supposed to be a children's movie, which would explain the silliness of it...? I'm honestly confused by the good ratings -- are they all from fans of the book who just loved the movie as a visual companion to the novel?

title: This is absolutely NOT an 8 or above, lol.
If you read the book, beware
An hour and a half too long!
review: Oof.This was all over the place tonally. The underlying idea is the death of most of the universe. Big, huge, epic. But then it's just an unending string of the cringiest middle school jokes.Coincidentally, I love terrible "dad jokes", bad puns, etc., but these jokes were too low brow for me.The characters were largely paper thin.I've seen very long movies where it totally makes sense that everything needed to be there and cutting would have lost something important. This movie is the opposite. There are so many pointless scenes and scenes that go on too long.Also, there were more music breaks than a Hindi movie. Sooo many extended music sequences that were tortuous to sit through.The campy interaction between the two main characters was one step below Flight Of The Navigator.Then the ending. I mean endings. There were at least 5 endings. A scene wrapped everything up and the story was done. But then they added a last minute twist. Then they wrapped it up and twisted it again and again. It was unnecessary and exhausting. Especially after all of the aforementioned bloat, having the movie extended when I've already suffered enough was just cruel.

title: Overhyped nonsense
review: I really don't understand the hype and the hysterical praise heaped upon this very mediocre sci-fi thriller. It tries in vain to marry a humourless, pretentious Christopher Nolan premise to the happy-go-lucky audience-friendly style of an MCU movie and as a result falls flat in between. The rambling screenplay goes on and on and on, with ever shifting stakes and awkward exposition. Just when you think it's all over the film pulls a Peter Jackson and adds on a couple more needless endings. The actors try their best with what they have to work with, but even a powerhouse like Sandra Hüller seems to struggle with her cliche-ridden lines. The kitschy music is execrable and evokes the worst Hans Zimmer excesses. The film is far less intelligent than the makers (and many movie bros) seem to think. There are many films threading the same water that are far superior. Avoid.

title: Boring movie & untrue to the book
Why do people like this movie?
Too slow and long
review: It was ok but ultimately a bit dull with lots of glaring plot holes and weird music. Didn't know if it wanted to be Interstellar or ET. I don't really get why people liked it so much. Ryan Gosling's performance was good but not enough to carry the whole movie. Ultimately I wouldn't recommend it myself.

title: didn't hold up to the book
review: As the title says. The casting was horrible they made useless changes added useless things, did things that made no sense. This was a hard sci-fi book turned into a family friendly drama... Like basically all of the science elements were removed.Just so much time wasted with Ryan doing nothing where in the book grace is way smarter, way more interesting and far less lazy.All and all I wish I had not gone to see it.

title: Reduced to a boy and his dog.
review: I mean it was very pretty, but past that you have to be ok with the elimination of almost all the science, character development, and problems identified and solved on earth to like this movie. I got to the end of the movie and said to my wife "That was a ridiculous movie!" To which she replying "Rocky was cute!" SMH Basically it's a 3 hour movie, loosely based on characters from a book I like, devoid of most everything that made the book so good. Now I know why Rocky was in the trailer.

title: Project Hail Mary - Lost in Space, Lost in Story
I rarely walk out of a movie. Tonight, I did.
review: I went in expecting a serious, intelligent sci-fi experience. Instead, what I got was a strangely paced, tonally confused film that tries very hard to be charming and funny-but never quite earns it. The humor feels forced, the dialogue drags, and the film leans far too heavily on one long, drawn-out interaction that goes nowhere for what feels like an eternity.The core issue is pacing. For nearly an hour, the story stalls. There is no real escalation, no tension building, no sense of urgency. Just repetitive exchanges that are meant to feel clever or endearing but end up feeling indulgent and exhausting. At some point, I found myself wondering: is anything actually happening here?The performance doesn't help. Ryan is clearly talented, but here he comes across as trying too hard-constantly pushing a quirky, self-aware tone that clashes with what should be a high-stakes, end-of-the-world scenario. It breaks immersion rather than enhancing it.Visually, the film is also disappointing. The spacecraft movement and zero-gravity sequences feel oddly cartoonish, lacking the weight and realism that make great sci-fi believable. Instead of being immersed, I was constantly reminded that I was watching something artificial.This might work for viewers who enjoy slow, dialogue-driven stories with a light, almost playful tone. But if you're expecting tension, momentum, and a compelling build-up, this film will likely frustrate you.I gave it one hour. That was enough.

title: Boring Boring Boring
review: I went to watch this movie due to the high ratings by critics and viewers. That was a huge mistake. Oh my gosh it was way to long and boring. It was nearly 3 hours long. If I had not been with a friend I would have walked out. It reminded me of the Tom Hanks Movie Cast Away where he spent the entire time talking to a volley ball.

title: I don't understand the hype?? Project snoozefest
Science fiction for Dummies
review: I found this film boring enough to warrant a walk out half way through.The comedic tone was a failed attempt to mask "painful exposition dumps and the whole affair felt surprisingly flat despite the core premise - the exposition with flashbacks was too long. The interaction of Ryan Gosling (annoying) character with Rocky put some life back into the movie but one had to jump across the huge logic gaps and trust the journey which I did not as I couldn't care less by that time.

title: Disappointing. I really wish they read more of the book
review: Why do adapters have to dumb down a brilliant source material? Dumb down and yuck it up. Andy Weir is so good at the science in his science fiction and if this production team thought they were making a science fiction movie, they left out too much science. Add in the changes they made to the characters, which made less than no sense, especially with what they did to Stratt. The changes they made to the plot? Unnecessary, unless the objective was to dumb down to their target audience.

title: Pigs In Space!
review: Don't get me wrong-I love The Muppet Show. But this movie promised real science fiction, not a kiddie flick. It's the most sustained rapidfire of one-liners I've ever been subjected to. They never end.The space crisis movie may have seen its day, because there was nothing new here. It's gotten tired watching an astronaut swap out sunglasses and a beach hat while sipping vodka on a spaceship. The cute factor was laid on so thick in service of keeping us comfortable that there was never any strangeness, fear, tension, escalation, or danger. Zero cost. People keep calling it hopeful. What exactly was this movie hoping for?One thing I'd never expect to have to tell a professional storyteller: don't have your hero label themselves "brave." Especially not preceding a flashback showing that same hero induced into a coma because he was too afraid to board the ship. Weird choice.He was tossed onto a spaceship, sent light years into orbit, woke up decades later never having trained for zero gravity or a spacewalk-and yet he's crawling along the exterior of the hull while skimming the fiery atmosphere of an alien planet. There is no universe where that logic produces a decent narrative.I don't remember ever seeing a film where every person involved-actors, director, writer-seemed so self-aware that no unified effort emerged to make a great film. Just a lot of posturing. If juvenile tech bros are your thing, you'll love it.

title: Did I miss something?
review: There are not many movies I feel inclined to walk out of. This is such a movie. Despite the promotional machine going into overdrive, this is one of the most bloated, shambling, cliché inclusive movies that a creative team could possibly make. And creative they are, despite the utterly predictable story line There were passages where I sat there going 'oh no, here we go again, now we need the key: cute alien, near death experiences, vivid lights...'. All of our party (yes, not 14 to 18 segement) were underwhelmed scoring it 3/10. About 2hrs 40 minutes. Sigh. 😬

title: What's going on with movies these days?
review: The movie is stupid. The plot is awful. The jokes are rotten. Looks like they took all possible references available, mixed them in a blender, and vomited a sequence of non-sense stupidity made for captivating a public absolutely devoid of critical capacity. Brain rot made for people to watch while doom scrolling on their couches. Again, a white yankee saves the world, underestimating another beings. OMG, USA, they are SO GOOD AND SO NICE. Only this time it's not only muscles but nerdy too. Come on, give me a break.

title: Dark Comedy
review: It's probably just me because I see the average rating of over 8 out of 10. There is something off to me about a film that is presented to be about a rather serious and intriguing sci fi topic like the Sun dying but the first 30 minutes of the movie the audience (not everybody) is laughing at the antics mainly of Gosling's character. I don't get that mix of let's be funny and light while all life is in danger of perishing on the Earth because of the failing Sun. Maybe when you are a fan of real sci fi classics like 2001: A Space Odyssey this if just not your kind of movie. If you are a purist in that way you'll likely be disappointed with a very long movie. You could have left out the first 30 minutes and not lost a thing. Not a fan.

title: Too corny to be funny. Not interesting enough to be good sci fi
The Critique: Project Hail Mary
review: *Project Hail Mary* is a hollow, self-indulgent disaster-a vapid spectacle that flails for the grandeur of *Interstellar* but collapses into a black hole of its own making. It is a parade of clichés wrapped in scientific window dressing, reducing human ingenuity to a preachy, saccharine slog supported by laughably undercooked effects.The film exemplifies Hollywood's "emotional sludge" era: a formulaic cycle where syrupy sentiment and "save the world" stakes replace actual ideas. Subtiveity is dead, replaced by a marketable version of the unknown that turns alien intelligence into cuddly, emotionally legible constructs for a pre-packaged "moment." It isn't science fiction; it's a therapy session with better lighting.Even the protagonist is a tired caricature-a bumbling, man-child "genius" whose intellect is signaled by chaotic energy and lazy wardrobe tropes rather than rigorous method. Ultimately, the film treats complex physics like relativity as decorative props rather than narrative foundations. It is a hollow imitation of depth where spectacle substitutes for imagination and recycled tropes are sold as fresh.

title: Another science-fiction movie that doesn't deserve to have the prefix 'science' in its title
review: Well, I don't know how much this latest achievement deserves a comment, but we got another science-fiction movie that doesn't deserve to have the prefix 'science' in its title. So, it's pure fantasy, for the level of children of about preschool age.Any comparisons to "2001: A Space Odyssey" or "Interstellar" are extremely inappropriate. The fact that Ryan Gosling is a shade more likable actor than Matthew McConaughey doesn't change the fact that "Interstellar" was a good movie, unlike this one, which is the complete opposite.I have no desire to comment on premises such as living organisms inhabiting the stars, and for some magical reason need CO2, or that a high school teacher is the appropriate profile to save the world with his mystical knowledge and abilities. For the same reason, I did not consider it appropriate to read a prose work in the original, which was written solely for the purpose to became a script for Hollywood, by the author of the commercially successful "The Martian", in which the main premise is storm on the planet Mars, although for all that we do not know about Mars, we at least know from the beginning that no storms are possible there.If you really want to watch this movie, and don't mind the lead actor's vapid so-called acting, fine, but don't expect too much.

title: Bland child humor, I'm shocked about the positive reviews
review: Bland child humor, I'm shocked about the positive reviewsI don't mind childish humor, I was the biggest SpongeBob fan but this film was absolutely terrible, boring and unfunny.The alien has the persona and script of the UP dog, which was UNBEARABLE.First half hour was not bad but the last 2 hours torture and I really wanted to leave the cinema, but didn't want to bother my partner who was with me...he realized soon after what a terrible typical movie plot this is...I was thinking, no this can't be this bad, it has to get better but NOPE. Utter disrespect to peoples life time.

title: Project Braindead
review: Overhyped to the max. Only if you like Grogu and think he is a cool addition to the Star Wars universe might this show be right up your street, and if E. T. is still your favorite movie of all time... To me, Project Hail Mary is mostly just boring, and the logic issues are as big as the Milky Way - and beyond. Anyway, it may hit the right buttons with kids, so if you have no better ideas, you can have some family time with it. But quality time is something else in my opinion. Anyway, production is solid, Gosling too.

title: Almost sci-fi - just missing the science
review: I watched Project Hail Mary and honestly, I didn't like it at all in terms of its genre.It's supposed to be science fiction, but the "science" part is almost completely butchered. The movie constantly ignores the basic rules of physics, and there's very little that feels grounded or intellectually satisfying. If you're someone who enjoys realistic, science-driven sci-fi, this will be a huge disappointment.The standout of the film is clearly Sandra Hüller. Her performance is outstanding-she carries the heavier emotional scenes with real skill and presence. I'd definitely like to see her in more films within this genre.On the other hand, Ryan Gosling's character feels far too loose and unserious. He's constantly trying to be funny. To be fair, there are a few genuinely funny moments, and some of them do land. However, the constant push for humor quickly becomes forced and repetitive, which takes away from the overall experience.The fictional concept itself is actually quite original and interesting. However, it's buried under scientific inaccuracies and an overreliance on humor. The tone ends up feeling more like a teenager-oriented sci-fi rather than a mature, thought-provoking one.The only real similarity it shares with Interstellar is that both take place in space-and that's about it.

title: Very disappointed in the movie adaptation
Could have done better
review: I had not read the book so I went in with an open mind. I found the movie too long and a bit silly. They were seemingly trying to convey the complexity of the human experience but it just came up short. As with many films today they just had too many endings and it dragged along. The score was mismatched, loud and grating. The could have done better.

title: insultingly long, painfully dumbed down
review: It started off like it respected me. That's what makes this worse.The first stretch actually worked. Smart setup, real stakes, and a tone that didn't talk down to the audience. It felt like it trusted you to keep up. You settle in thinking you're getting something grounded, maybe even a serious sci-fi entry worth paying attention to.Then it pivots-hard. What started as thoughtful turns into something that feels like it was rewritten for kids halfway through production. The dialogue simplifies, the tension evaporates, and every interesting edge gets sanded down. By the third act, it's not even pretending anymore.And it just. Keeps. Ending.You get one ending, then another, then another. Each one feels like it should've been the last, and yet it keeps dragging itself back up for one more forced emotional beat. At some point you're not watching the movie-you're waiting for it to finally stop.This isn't a theatrical movie. It's streaming filler stretched past its limits. The kind of thing you half-watch on a couch while checking your phone, not something worth sitting through in a theater."Best movie of the year"? No chance. Not even close.Skip it.

title: I really wanted to like it.
review: If you want to see an intense, thought-provoking, otherworldly sci-fi movie, dig out your old Bladerunner DVD. However, if you want to see a quirky rom-com about the growing relationship between a man and an animated rock, oh boy are you in for a treat.There's precisely zero tension generated at the outset because everyone is glib and firing off quips about the impending fate of humanity, therefore it's impossible to care about the outcome of the mission.There's also absolutely none of the otherworldly atmosphere that should be a given in an sci-fi movie. Most of this is set inside a generic spaceship or in a dim corridor between two spaceships. It has all the immersive ambience of a branch of Tesco.But by far its biggest mistake is the tone. Ryan Gosling's "lovably-hapless everyman" is supposed to be charming and his new rock friend is basically Paddington Bear except he's a rock. It's as if they don't believe the audience can be trusted with scientific information so they jam it full of "light-hearted" moments to keep them engaged.I'm pleased that the film has been a big hit at the cinema because anything that keeps cinemas open is good. However, as T2 and umpteen other sci-fi blockbusters showed, audiences will pay to see challenging, atmospheric sci-fi and don't need a jaunty, quirky romcom-style tone to be able to appreciate a story.

title: Beavis and Butt-Head do Interstellar
review: Ryan Gosling plays himself. No characterization. Wafer thin plot. Stupid science. Beavis and Butt-Head humour. The only redeeming factors were Sandra Hüller who played her deadpan character with finesse, and the AI graphics which were epic. Interstellar, Gravity, The Martian are much, much better films.

title: Seems like they hoped the production design and score would fill the gaps.
review: I never read the book, but went with someone who did. He too felt this was pretty poor. As a Lord and Miller fan, to me, this was cold lasagna. It should have been humorous comfort food, but instead was overwrought, and yet lacking. How does that happen? Seems like they hoped the production design and score would fill the gaps. PD was nice, but too much for the flimsiness of the whole. Meanwhile, the score went nuclear trying to enforce emotion that just wasn't there, in the movie or the audience.

title: Would have been better with a Moon twist
Boring as hell
review: Way too long,almost every scene dragged on longer than it needed to. Probably could've cut an hour out of the movie in editing. The rock alien was a dumb part of the movie. Very emotion bait, and the idea of his teaching it english is idiotic. There was a ton of stupid things in the movie that took me out of the film. Extremely hard to get immersed in the cinema when I literally was rolling my eyes throughout the movie. Overglazed and a low key chore to sit through.

title: More Childish Than Clever: A Sci-Fi Letdown
review: There are sci-fi films that are childish, yet still intelligent and engaging-like WALL-E. But Project Hail Mary is definitely not one of them. The film is childish, but with no real story and rather silly jokes. I haven't read or listened to the book, so I don't know what the original story is like, but the movie is meaningless for true science fiction fans.If you have a child around 7-8 years old and want to introduce them to sci-fi, then this film might be relatively okay. But for older sci-fi lovers-skip it...Honestly, it even feels a bit embarrassing to call this science fiction.

title: Too lighthearted and off the mark
review: The tone of this movie is off; it's a mixed bag between trying to be a modern ET and an Intersteller but those premises clash the entire movie. Moments of talk about human extinction are directly following by goofy jokes that completely missed their mark.Ryan Gosling is also trying way too hard making this movie extremely cringy. After an hour and the introduction of a squeaking walking space alien rock I walked out of the theatre.

title: Nonsensical tripe
review: The narrative structure of this film is disjointed, jumping across timelines in a way that feels both literal and figurative. It leans heavily on high-end visual effects, the lead actor's charisma, and a string of lackluster jokes to carry the weight.Ultimately, even the most polished aesthetics can't compensate for a weak story; I found myself repeatedly checking the clock, wondering when the credits would finally roll. Trimming the runtime by at least 30 minutes would have gone a long way in fixing the sluggish pacing.Rock Buddy was used more as a 1 dimensional prop rather than a character, which felt insincere.

title: Perfect if you dont like movies
review: Apocalypse, but make it cozy.Scene after scene that just... happens. The dialogue, or any interaction for that matter, is so poorly written it hurts.No buildup, no weight. The sun is dying and Gosling's doing goofy bits, like tripping on stuff in a space ship. Are you for real?! Extinction-level stakes, Saturday morning cartoon energy. Turns out saving humanity is mostly puns and good vibes.21k reviews and the same rating as Interstellar only shows the level of quality we've succumbed to.

title: Incredibly bad.
review: I cannot understand how this movie got 8.4 at the time of reading. This is basically the biggest ripoff of Interstellar, but like a 100 times worse. The story is very similar, obviously Rocky is Tar. So many scene are just COPIED from Interstellar. The acting is bad. The writing of the characters is terrible. The music makes NO SENSE. Rock, then classical, then Maori, then Spanish, then... the lion king? Am I watching a Disney movie? All gratuitous with ZERO link to story or characters. And the CONSTANT goofy humour that does not stop for a second takes away absolutely everything that could potentially be a little bit good of this movie. And it's not even funny unless you're 12yo maybe? What a disappointment. I wasted 156 min of my life for this. If I watched it at home, I would have stopped after 10min.Absolutely not worth the praise. Crazy.

title: The return of the Man-child
review:
But big-budget films these days often seem designed to discourage independent thought. Image and sound, dialogue and music, editing and camera movement - all work overtime to bypass analytical reasoning and induce a state of pure sensation, ensuring we feel precisely what they want us to feel at any given moment.They seem to have forgotten Ernst Lubitsch's famous advice: "Let the audience add up two plus two. They'll love you forever."
Instead, filmmakers spoon-feed answers before we've even had the chance to form a single question.Hollywood has lost the plot, confusing sensation with storytelling and noise with meaning. They've forgotten how to trust the stories they tell, abandoning logic for spectacle and hoping we won't look too closely at the seams.

title: Too light and too frantic to leave a real impact.
review: Too light and too frantic to leave a real impactI went into Project Hail Mary with fairly high expectations, but ultimately left the theater feeling surprisingly unmoved. Despite the film's obvious ambition, it never managed to engage me emotionally. None of the events, no matter how significant they were meant to be, truly pulled me in or stayed with me.One of the main reasons for this is the constant use of humor. While many of the comedic moments are well executed and genuinely funny, they are simply too frequent. The film rarely allows scenes to breathe or carry emotional weight. For a big science fiction blockbuster, I expected more restraint and seriousness at times. Instead, it often felt like I was watching a romantic comedy that just happens to take place in space.Visually, I also struggled to connect with the story. The editing is very fast and flashy, making it hard to fully process what is happening on screen. As a result, I never really felt immersed in the world of the film, which is a major drawback for a story of this scope.Finally, I expected the technical aspects to be more solid overall. For a production of this scale, not everything felt as carefully thought out or convincing as it should have been, which created even more distance between myself and the story.Project Hail Mary will likely work for viewers who enjoy a consistently light tone and frequent humor, but for me it lacked the emotional depth, focus, and seriousness needed to truly make an impact.

title: Unbearable slapstick orgy
review: I expected what is sometimes called competency porn, like The Martian, Arrival, 90s Star Trek or classic SciFi literature a la Stanislaw Lem. Unfortunately, Project Hail Mary is the opposite. The film is not interested in the presented problems an its solutions and you're not supposed to think too hard about the science. I'm convinced that every real scientist finds a point in this movie that makes him want to leave the cinema - physicists, chemists, definitively linguists. That Grace is the only man for the job is laughable. This, together with the forced comedic, quippy dialog (I heard the book has the same problem) and the over-the-top soundtrack (both OST and licenced), makes the first half of the movie almost unbearable, a slapstick orgy more often than not.Admittedly, the second half is better, as the film becomes a buddy movie. So, if you're into themes of friendship and sacrifice, you'll find value here.However, it couldn't save the movie for me, and the aforementioned problems persist. If you're looking for good science fiction, look elsewhere.

title: Project Full of Drawn Out Needless Dialogue
Same modern manufactured "substance" as Sinners
review: This movie is a cultural extinction event. The whole ordeal plays like a goofy montage or a Tiktok reel? For the life of me I cannot grasp how audiences around the world are calling this a pop culture phenomenon other than it being manufactured and paid for alter-internet-reality hype. You can't even trust movie reviews anymore. What happened to creating something and releasing it to the world without telling audiences what they should think and feel? To compare this film to Interstellar is like comparing Harry Styles to David Bowie - an utter joke!

title: Corny & cute
review: Not a great sci-fi movie. I was expecting Aliens and got The Muppets. Cute for kids, but corny overall. Rocky is well played. He's a puppet as opposed to CGI. The beginning of the movie was boring. I fell asleep, and so did my friend, but once the story evolved it was interesting. Gosling's acting was so so.

title: Boring movie
review: The film was truly drawn-out; scenes that could have been done in 2 minutes were stretched to 30 minutes.Can't believe someone said its cinema masterpiece, but when I watch it, everything just flowed by - a completely flat film, there was absolutely no subtlety in the filmmaking at all.For the first 40 minutes, I still felt like it held potential, making me believe I was about to watch a fascinating story filled with wondrous cosmic scientific theories... But no, the film's theoretical premise was too superficial, and the resolution was the same. So, if anyone compares it to 'Interstellar,' it really doesn't measure up.The more I watched, the more it felt like I'm watching a child learning to read. Then, they kept stuffing in and repeating cheesy bromance scenes that were exhausting to watch.Ryan Gosling absolutely had to look handsome from beginning to end, no matter how perilous the situation. If this guy isn't directed by a good director, his fake emotional weaknesses become glaringly obvious.

title: Too long and childish
review: I expected this movie would have a more serious tone, but every scene is designed with some slightly comedic ending. Not actually funny, just kinda meh. The movie is also very predictable, ineffective in creating any level of suspense and stacked with way too many unnecessary scenes adding to it's light tone.

title: Surprise letdown
review: I went along with the hype on this one, given the near universal praise it seemed like it's been getting, and might've gone anyway given my fandom of Science Fiction (when it's good) and the mad skills of Ryan Gosling. So I was astonished to find myself growing bored after just the first 15 minutes. Naturally I expected things to pick up but they never did that much. Another thing that bummed me out was that IMDB has it down as a "dark comedy" which it most definitely is not. The comedy is slapstick and mostly cute, better suited to the family genre, which it should perhaps be considered as, more than the thought provoking sci-fi flick with a nice dollop of dark comedy mixed in that I'd walked in hoping to see.

title: The Most Overrated Sci-Fi Film in Years
review: I genuinely don't understand the hype around Project Hail Mary.This was supposed to be a high-stakes, intelligent sci fi film about saving humanity. Instead, it plays out like a quirky, lighthearted experiment that never fully commits to its own premise. The tone is completely off. You have some of the brightest minds on Earth dealing with an extinction-level crisis, yet the dialogue is filled with sarcastic, awkward humor that feels out of place and, at times, downright cringe.Ryan Gosling's performance didn't work for me either. He felt miscast, and the film also leans heavily into trying to build a deep emotional connection between his character and a visually uninspired looking rock-like being, and it just felt silly rather than meaningful. If you enjoy lighthearted sci fi with E. T.-style charm and moments that feel more like they belong in a kids movie, you might appreciate this approach. For me, it completely undercut the seriousness of the story.The pacing drags, the emotional weight never lands, and the soundtrack is completely forgettable. Not a single moment gave me chills or elevated the experience, which is a huge miss for a sci fi film.What really blows my mind is seeing people compare this to Interstellar. That film is on another level in every category. This isn't even in the same conversation.I wanted to like this. I expected to like this. But in the end, it felt cheesy, overlong, and forgettable.This might be one of the most overrated movies I've seen in a long time.

title: This adaptation was made for kids
review: Just finished the book and loved it.Regarding the movie, I thought the humor was marvel-esk. Well executed but just turned the movie into a comedy. Felt like the book was written for adults and the movie is aimed towards kids. So much science was left out. Some of it felt critical to the plot. The problem solving dialogue in the book between Dr. Grace and Rocky helped develop the bond for all the emotional beats later. Without that dialogue Rocky became the quirky sidekick, displaying cutesy mannerisms. While I'm sitting there thinking, "Rocky is supposed to be a 200 year old alien engineer". They kept trying to force feed us emotional moments with heavy soundtrack usage, but they just didn't nail the pacing for it to work.

title: Project Boredom
review: I'm calling it now, this will be the most overrated movie of 2026. Don't get me wrong, i don't usually complain about films being dragged out and being boring, as that is lazy criticism, but i was always was on the brink of falling asleep. Everything about the movie just didn't work for me, the long winded scientific jargon, the monotonous flashbacks, and most importantly the overly childish contact with Rocky the alien. I just really felt the runtime here, and there were so many times i thought the movie was going to end ... but it just kept going on. I've always found Ryan Gosling relying on his charm, rather than fine acting and the charm wears thin very quickly here. I understand why others really enjoyed this movie, i wish i did too, but for some reason it just didn't click with me at all.

title: Not made for fans of the book
review: I feel like that is the important point to make from my perspective, because as someone who has been waiting for years for this to come out it did not meet my hopes. My first problem was that Ryland Grace was reduced to a bumbling doofus, and the humor was on a level for 8 year olds. Now admittedly, some of the humor in the book was juvenile, but more like science nerd juvenile. The humor in this felt more like it was written for Pixar audiences, complete with Fortnite dances, and the few jokes from the book were repeated ad nauseum. I did not like the voice acting for Rocky. It made no sense that the computer translator would pronounce the word "human" like a Ferengi or imitate Sylvester Stallone. That's more of a gripe with whoever directed him than the actor himself. I also did not like the soundtrack, it was like a very stock, manufactured drama type soundtrack and never felt connected to the plot. That is when they weren't just lazily using old pop songs. They also wasted Stratt, who was a great character and they had the perfect casting for. And reducing Rocky's musical language to a bunch of stock audio of monkeys, pigs, and birds? Come on.My biggest issue is that Andy Weir went to painstaking lengths to create this theoretically plausible scientific scenario that was deeply involved and very well thought out, and they reduced it to a bunch of slapstick humor and montages ending with Ryan Gosling doing fist pumps to gloss over all of the actual science.I will say the visual design department was on point. They had some cool material to work with and knocked it out of the park. Rocky looked perfect, and the planet Adrian was awesome. I particularly liked the way they did the transparent Xenonite, that was really creative. I would have just used plexiglass.Overall it was an entertaining movie, but it let me down as someone who really enjoyed the book. It seems like they just dumbed it down so it would appeal to kids.

title: Sadly it doesn't 'Rock'
review: I wanted to like this movie. Andy Weir adaptation, Gosling, Lord and Miller directing. On paper, it should have been a slam dunk. But I left the theater genuinely disappointed given all that the book had to offer.The biggest problem is tone. This is a story about an extinction-level crisis, a one-way suicide mission to save humanity. That should carry weight. Instead, the film plays like a quirky buddy comedy that never commits to its own premise. The humor feels forced, the dialogue drags, and the playful, self-aware tone completely undercuts any real stakes.Gosling is talented, but he felt misdirected here. He leans so hard into charming and quippy that it clashes with what should be life-or-death moments and true existential crises. The scenes with Rocky walk a fine line between endearing and corny, and for me they mostly landed on the wrong side. It felt less like a compelling sci-fi page turner like the book, and more like a kids' movie in sci-fi clothing.At two and a half hours, the pacing is brutal. There's a long stretch where the story just stalls, with repetitive exchanges meant to feel clever that end up feeling indulgent. The sci-fi elements felt like recycled ideas from The Martian and Interstellar without the rigor of either. The VFX had moments that felt oddly cartoonish, and the soundtrack was forgettable.If you want a lighthearted, feel-good space adventure, this delivers. But if you walked in expecting serious, intelligent sci-fi with real tension like the book, you'll probably leave feeling like they should have just re-released The Martian and saved everyone the trouble.

title: Epic disappointment
review: This had the potential to be a great movie. Andy Weir being a great Sci-Fi writer with a meticulous attention scientific detail and the success of the Martian had my hopes up.However, everything that makes an Andy Weir novel is completely missing in this movie. The scientific detail is not just lacking it is non-existent. Discoveries and explanations that are vital to the story are glossed over in a lightening fashion.Instead the movie is filled with fluff and various attempts at humour, but falls flat.Granted it is a difficult story to explain on the screen, but not even attempting it is inexcusable.So what you are left with is a movie that is neither here nor there and wont really satisfy anyone, but particularly not Andy Weir fans.

title: Flat and Boring
review: I can't understand the hype of this movie. There was no excitement and the story felt flat and boring. I truly can't believe how highly rated it is. I almost felt like walking out. They also could have made it 2 hours. It just dragged on. You can pick literally any other astronaut movie and that movie is probably better. If you want to watch a guy talk to a rock for 3 hours and find that entertaining then go right ahead. The worst movie of 2026 so far.

title: Not quite bad enough to walk out on, but close.
review: This is not a good movie, unless you are 14.A weird mash up of Arrival, Interstellar and Castaway, and it did not have the gravitas of the former two, nor the character development of the latter. It makes no sense for such a moron to be on a space flight, global emergency or not, the orbital manoeuvres were straight up clown show, and the only thing that kept the movie going was the string of juvenile gags every 10 seconds. Terrible movie and I'm not sure where all this love is coming from.Currently rated at 8.4 so it's beating out the likes of Lawrence of Arabia, Reservoir Dogs, Scarface and 2001 A Space Odyssey. Sorry, but nuh-uh. Shame on these reviewers.Saw it on 70mm Imax and the cinematography and sound design were good. Gosling did alright with what he had.

title: Disappointment...
review: I saw "Project Hail Mary" at a cinema in Dalat in English. I haven't been so disappointed in a long time. 😐. Instead of a profound, dramatic, and atmospheric work, we were treated to a children's fantasy comedy with songs, dances, and melodramatic moments. As a standalone children's film, it's not bad. But if you've read the book, everything you see in the film adaptation seems like cheap farce. The special effects are also very average, and in places, downright mediocre.My rating: 51 out of 100.

title: Crashing Bore
review: I almost walked out of this movie twice because I was so bored and uninterested. But I soldiered on, hoping the ending would somehow improve the story. It didn't. Rocky's and Grace's relationship and communication left me SMH. The "classroom" scene was ridiculous. Even considering the necessary suspension of disbelief for a sci-fi movie, I found it preposterous.

title: Average
review: Project Hail Mary was, for me, a very average movie and nothing more. I would give it a 5/10. It is not a bad film in the sense that it is unwatchable, but it also never becomes exciting, memorable, or emotionally engaging enough to stand out. The movie had a premise that sounded interesting at first, but the final result did not live up to the idea behind it.The story was my biggest disappointment. Instead of feeling fresh and gripping, it felt weak and underdeveloped in several places. I kept waiting for it to build real tension or deliver a stronger emotional impact, but it never fully got there. The pacing also made the experience feel longer than it should have been, and some parts felt like they were trying too hard without actually creating interest. I just could not connect with the plot the way I hoped I would.The acting was another reason the movie fell flat for me. The performances were not terrible, but they also were not convincing enough to make me care deeply about the characters. Some scenes that should have felt powerful came across as flat or awkward instead. That made it harder to become invested in what was happening on screen.I also did not like the alien design. Instead of looking impressive, interesting, or even slightly unsettling in a good way, it looked strange in a way that pulled me out of the movie. For a film like this, the alien should be one of the most memorable parts, but here it just did not work for me.Overall, Project Hail Mary was just okay. It had potential, but the story, acting, and alien look all kept it from being something better. A decent one-time watch, but nothing I would strongly recommend.

title: If martian and interstellar were combined into a worst movie
review: This movie was way longer than it should have been ryan gosling character was frustrating to watch of how bad his attempts at being funny. Movie gets really boring and the suspense never actually builds up. Do not recommend to watch unless you want a corny children's movie also no science was explained in this movie.

title: If you read and loved the book, don't expect much from this movie.
review: So, no spoiler alert...I walked out of the movie. They dumbed down Grace so much that it annoyed me every time he was on screen. The book was intelligent and witty. Grace was described as a cool and intelligent teacher. His obstacle to overcome was that he was scared. When he awoke from his coma, he immediately started to process and go through things scientifically. The movie version was some bumbling idiot approach. Yes, he figures things out, but at no point did I feel that he was putting any effort into anything. They made rocky be the "baby Yoda" trope. The added so much nonsense, like his video logs to be funny. It was just a total miss on what made the book special.In the Martian, they captured what smart, funny, and witty could be. They made the science fun. This movie seems like a cash grab. I got up to use the restroom, and found myself not caring about going back in. So I went home instead of sitting through the rest.

title: Trading mystery for bland hollywood comedy
review: I had such high hopes for this, especially since the source material is one of the best sci-fi novels I've read in years. Unfortunately, the adaptation misses the mark. Ryan Gosling feels like a "star" casting choice rather than a fit for the character's soul, and the forced, exaggerated humor becomes grating almost immediately. I came for the mystery, reverence, and the awe of deep space, not a comedy. A disappointing tonal shift that sacrifices atmosphere for accessibility.

title: Temu Interstellar & Arrival
review: Didn't enjoy it as much as most people apparently. I expected an emotional, intelligent movie, but I thought it was cheesy and predictable. Something to watch while you don't have to pay full attention. It's an original story and the acting was great. Overall I wasn't really invested because of the lack of emotional depth.

title: Second half was meh. Ending was so cheesy.
review: Sadly...We were disappointed. Lots of promise in first 1/3. Turned super kiddish. I guess I love more intense thriller sci-fi and movies in general but was not expecting it to be as cheesy as it was. The ending was like what. Laaaaaame.I mean I did tear up at one part but that doesn't make it an epic movie in my mind. Definitely PG feel good movie vs the thriller they push in the previews/marketing.

title: Family Friendly space opera, amusing but not memorable
NOT GOOD
review: I do not know how this movie rates so high! I think I am being generous by giving 5 stars. -- The high ratings must be from bots/fake.Unless people saw a movie that I did not see. The movie and the characters we not engaging. The move moved at a snails pace.Iwould not recommend this movie to anybody.

title: 150-minute sitcom
review: Project Hail Mary is technically polished and visually impressive but it feels significantly overhyped. Despite its high ratings the movie plays out more like a goofy sitcom than an epic science fiction drama. The tone is frequently undermined by forced humor and cringeworthy moments which strip away any real sense of stakes or gravitas. The film relies heavily on cliches borrowed from better movies but it fails to capture their emotional weight. The portrayal of the modern savior as an isolated individual with no family or deep sacrifices makes his journey feel hollow and unearned. Furthermore the simplified and almost infantile language used for communication reduces a monumental first contact to the level of a children cartoon. Ultimately it is a visually stunning but intellectually thin experience that prioritizes lighthearted escapism over genuine human transformation. In the end he saves the Earth but clearly does not find it worth living on choosing instead to stay in a cage in an alien world where his ego and self worth are never challenged.

title: Too silly
review: I really liked The Martian. It was smart, intelligent and naturally funny. Suspension of disbelief was never tested.I can't say the same about Project Hail Mary. It is incredibly well made, but I had a hard time believing in what I saw. The movie is simply too goofy, trying too much to be funny, in a juvenile way. The story lose gravitas, and end up being boring - especially due to the movies length. It is way too long!The science part never gets interesting, like in The Martian, and it never made me think, like the best sci fi movies make you do. It just is and seems kinda random. Mostly made for giggles.The movie is too polished to really make an impact. There are no real surprises, no real sense of danger and the humour is more miss than hit.I can't say that I am dissapointed, as the trailer didn't intrigue me at all, but when the favorable reviews started pouring in, it made me hope that the movie would surprise me in a positive way. It didn't.

title: Pop sci-fi
review: "I put the not in astronaut!"Subtitled, me and my pet rock. I should probably tread carefully here, as I know this film is loved by many, including those I respect. And it's certainly not without its charms either, featuring a cute alien, lots of humor, and a story revolving around friendship. The notes of finding one's courage under great pressure and what it means to sacrifice oneself were nice too. It would make a great popcorn film for families, or those looking for wholesome fun.I wasn't immune to all of that, but I was disappointed in just how much the science fiction took a backseat in a story set in space. I don't want to pick through all of its contrived aspects (it is a fairly long list), but for a taste, the trouble started with just how easily the human was able to converse with the alien, and in full sentences. Solaris, this is not. And you mean he's going to take his helmet off based on a crude rendering of an oxygen atom from his new friend, when what we breathe is mostly nitrogen and he can't possibly know what the mix might be?
