And why does he need to go outside the spacecraft while "fishing" without first getting out of the other world's atmosphere. Etc etc. It wasn't the usual type of suspension of disbelief over a premise or two for the genre, it was necessary again and again, removing any semblance of realism or wonderment.To me the film felt a bit dumbed down to appeal to the mass market, and everything about it geared around a feel-good tone, not raising the profound questions that come from encountering alien life or being so far out in space. Even back on Earth, we have the head of the project mentioning her faith in God, and when there's a glimmer of darkness in how she gets the main character out in space, it doesn't go anywhere, it's smoothed over. There are no bad guys or bad intentions at all in this universe, no real menace. As pop cinema this definitely has entertainment value, but at least for my taste, it could have been so much better.

title: Feel good Sci-fi movie for kids
review: Didn't read the book. Watched the movie last night. I wish someone had told me this was a movie for kids with quirky characters. I was expecting a serious sci-fi movie. I mean it's a perfect feel-good movie with very predictable events seen a million times.The humour is mostly for kids (quirky lines and characters), the sound design of Rocky character is very much Pixar/Disney style just like the whole story. Also there's just too much quirkiness for my taste - almost every scene is milking some kind of comedic element. Most of them didn't land for me because I'm not a kid anymore (unfortunately).If you didn't read the book and if you're expecting a serious sci-fi movie like Interstellar/Arrival/2001 you're probably gonna be disappointed. People are hyping this movie like it's some kind of cinematic masterpiece - it's not. Think of this movie more like Interstellar/Arrival made for kids by Pixar.

title: What is the hype all about ?
review: Dont get me wrong, it is a very watchable fun movie. But i am ver surprised by alot of people calling it the best thing since sliced bread. What am i missing here ? Its an okay/fun movie with a good production, and thats about it. I didnt like The Martian either, maybe andy weir does not work on me.

title: The Thinking Happens Elsewhere
review: Fans of the novel will likely spend much of the film runtime wondering how a story built around scientific curiosity was turned into something so uninterested in it.Andy Weir's Project Hail Mary works because it's driven by curiosity. The novel is essentially a chain of observation, hypothesis, experiment, and discovery. The science isn't window dressing - it's the story.The film adaptation removes most of that and replaces it with two and a half hours of sentimental filler. Somehow the movie finds time for endless emotional padding but none for the reasoning that made the plot work in the first place. Problems appear, get solved , and move on with barely a hint of the intellectual puzzle that defined the book.What remains is a simplified, syrupy version of the story that seems deeply afraid the audience might have to think for a moment. The terrible score hammers every emotional beat so hard it becomes exhausting.

title: Let's pump the brakes a bit on the hype.
Too long for such a simple plot
review: A simple plot like this did not need a 2.5h movie. It seems like story telling has become a lost art these days in Hollywood. While the story was cute, it was boring and predictable. The movie was going back and forth between the present and the past slowly reveling the reason the lead was out in space, which most had probably figured out after 40min. This movie felt extremely dragged out and could have been wrapped up in under 2 hours.

title: Decent - but could/should have been EPIC
review: Don't get me wrong, it's still a good watch. But for those that read the book, you'll be left wanting.The beginning is rushed and the overall jeopardy of humanity's situation is glossed over entirely (Weir makes it way more apparent and detailed in the book). So much so that, when we went to see it, a couple arrived about 10 mins late (not ideal in any film) but they then probably sat for the following 2.5 hours without a clue as to why this was all so important.Ryan Reynolds is great, the jokes all land nicely and his relationship with Rocky is heartwarming. I did feel that there wasn't a huge amount of time for character development here. Grace isn't introduced well, Stratt only has a minor part. In the book their dynamic relationship is explored much more thoroughly.I couldn't help but compare this film to The Martian, a true modern great, also based on a book written by Andy Weir. Where that film blends science and humour perfectly, focusing on the former; this flips the script and is almost a pure comedy. The science is far less impressive in Project Hail Mary - the book was far more detailed and impressive in that regard. The film trades sci-fi drama for laughs.All in all - I think this was a missed opportunity. It could easily have been a 2-3 season TV epic. Or a 3 part trilogy. Instead, we were left with a ~3 hour mediocre entry. Which is just a shame more than anything.

title: Good if you didn't have high hopes
Disappointing
review: First of all, those who are comparing this to Interstellar or Martian need to get a mental check-up. Not that this movie is bad but it should be marketed as a kids/ Disney movie. There is absolutely no plot or thrill. It is a family summer time flick but some heart warming moments and good visuals. Average fare overall!

title: Ryan Gosling and a stellar buddy movie, but where is the grit?
review: Phil Lord and Christopher Miller deliver a visually stunning IMAX experience, but they struggle to balance the "hard science" of Andy Weir's novel with their trademark comedic flair. Ryan Gosling's immense charisma carries the film, turning Dr. Grace into a deeply relatable hero whose chemistry with the alien Rocky provides the story's true emotional core. However, Drew Goddard's script feels overlong at 156 minutes, and the constant flashbacks to Earth frequently derail the tension. While light years ahead of the current anemic and self-indulgent French dramas, Project Hail Mary remains a bit too "Disney" in its execution. It is a highly entertaining blockbuster, but it lacks the cold, calculated scientific rigor that made the book a masterpiece.

title: Good concept, but not very engaging
review: Interesting concept, but the execution didn't work for me. It leans heavily on technical problem-solving and never really delivers a strong visual or emotional payoff. I kept waiting for it to feel more immersive or exciting, but it stayed pretty flat throughout. I could have finished it, but ultimately didn't feel invested enough to stay.

title: Is it a space or a child movie?
review: In an overall view, the film is not really bad, is funny and it heals your boredom. BUT, it mix topics in a very unnatural way. The first hour of the move looks like is gonna be as realistic as possible, that until he meets the alien. Firstly, it seems that communication between them was gonna be a real issue, and, 2 minutes later, they're hanging out like bros and using Latinism, is a very big jump that breaks the continuity and coherence of the narrative. Sometimes it even seems like they took Interstellar and Guardians of the Galaxy and mixed them together in a blender.Go watch it with not high expectations.

title: Meh
review: They watered down the book, removed some more serious theme and darker jokes to make this PG-13 goofy buddy comedy. The book and the movie should be enjoyed separately. Don't expect much science, don't dwell too deep. Keep in mind that you wouldn't get much detailed explanations about "what's going on here" in the movie, and many of your questions raised during the movie will likely be left unanswered.And for the fans of the book? Congratulations! All your beloved quotes from the book will not be featured in the movie. Human word: Sarcasm. Yep, the 'sarcasm' scene was not in the movie either.Personally, I don't like it. Maybe I'm letting my appreciation for the book getting in the way. But still, I would't say it's as good as The Martian, Intersteller, Gravity, etc. Lower your expectations, grab a bucket of popcorn, expect something silly and funny. Trust me, you'll do just fine.

title: Good Book - Okay Movie
review: Sadly, I was quite disappointed. First, I strongly encourage reading the book prior to watching the movie. Now, I completely understand that most movies are unable to capture the full essence of a book. However, there is a lot missing from the film. First, it starts off too quickly. For instance, who is Eva Stratt? Who is Ryland Grace? And interestingly, Eva Stratt plays such a big role in the book; much more than what the movie portrays. There are no scenes involving the school children (you will understand if you read the book), Dr. Grace's connection with them, why he decides to commit himself to the project, his theory, etc. Yes, it is somewhat mentioned, but not enough to fully understand.Instead, it felt as though Hollywood producers decided to cut the film short to appeal to a younger audience - one that would be entertained by Rocky, the alien. However, the storyline is not intended to appeal to children (not that it can't, but it's not E. T.).While the book itself is not like "The Martian" and can appeal to a larger audience, they are vastly different.I do want to end on a positive note. The acting was phenomenal. I enjoyed seeing the emotional depth from Ryan Gosling. The emotions exhibited from the book was brought to the screen. Sandra Hüller did the same as well however, too much was either edited or the screenwriter chose to go in a different direction. However, I fail to see how Andy Weir would have signed off on screenplay - unless he believed it would generate higher book sales because fans would be intrigued to read the missing gaps.Lastly, I believe any film based on a novel should include the author(s) and title of the book in ALL marketing materials.

title: Four Culprits That Killed My Attention Spanned
review: Project Hail Mary is a visually breathtaking sci fi epic that delivers striking imagery, impressive world building, and a genuinely heartfelt core. The film's strongest moments come from its sense of scale the vastness of space, the intricate ship design, and the emotional intimacy of its central relationship. It's a movie that looks incredible and often feels ambitious in all the right ways.But despite its beauty, the film struggles with four major culprits that make it feel longer than it should.First, the rhythm never fully settles. The movie constantly shifts between isolation drama, scientific puzzle solving, comedic beats, and flashback storytelling. Instead of building momentum, the rhythm resets again and again, stretching the experience.Second, the explanation heavy approach interrupts the emotional flow. The film frequently pauses to break down scientific concepts, and while the ideas are interesting, the timing often undercuts tension rather than enhancing it.Third, the tone shifts create friction. Lighthearted humor sits beside existential stakes, and the contrast can be jarring. The movie never quite decides whether it wants to be a survival thriller, a buddy comedy, or a heartfelt drama.Finally, the pacing especially in the middle act lingers too long. The problem solving sequences are methodical and repetitive, slowing the film's forward drive.In the end, Project Hail Mary is imaginative and emotionally warm, but its uneven rhythm, explanations, tone shifts, and pacing issues keep it from fully soaring.My rating: 6/10.

title: I really ask myself how people rate movies nowadays
review: Most mid scifi movie of at least the last five years. I haven't seen something this bad in a while, and for sure not with this kind of budget. Could have been a 1.30h movie. Really inconsistent story, full of useless jokes mimicking "guardians of the galaxy" in really terrible way and wannabe plot-twist everyone with a little brain would have expected.Save a trip to the theater.

title: Wish I'd been told it was a kid's movie
review: Absolutely wonderful. For children. Minus the run time. I was expecting a smart and compelling Sci-fi movie like The Martian. But this movie was shallow with bad dialogue and incredibly forced humor. If you're into a buddy comedy with dialogue written for kids, this movie might be your jam. All of these 10 star reviews have to be paid for because there's no way this many people thought this film was any kind of masterpiece. Was it pretty to look at? Yes. Was there any kind of meaningful character backstory or development that made you care at all about what happened to them? Nope. I'd save the experience for home viewing.

title: Project Hail No
review: This trite, predictable flick required an entire truckload of disbelief suspension from the word go. The scientific community recruits a middle school teacher to conduct tests solo on a sample of something that the fate of the world depends on? I don't think so! The middle school teacher's students are interested, engaged, and care about something other than themselves? Sorry pal, that's not happening. This "scientist" knows how to operate the entire spaceship with minimal training? Nope!It's just The Martian repackaged as a buddy cop movie. I half expected to see a LLM given a screen credit as a writer. If this is on TV when I walk through the room in five years I will run until I'm out of earshot.

title: Project Wrap It Up Already Mary
review: A perfectly good film marred somewhat by over staying it's welcome. Ryan Gosling plays a scientist who gets enlisted to save the world. In the process he meets a space alien. And not a cute one. Not evil by any means, but they won't be selling dolls of this thing, that's for sure.But, as mentioned, the film's primary problem is it going on and on. I never read the book so I don't know if the original source material also has the film's conclusion but it shouldn't. From a movie goers point of view the end felt like the director was desperate to have a very specific ending that would not upset box office receipts too much. Ironic that the alien isn't commercial but that ending sure is. I mean, the amount of stretch to get us where the picture finally concludes is really something else.

title: Disappointing
review: I saw 8.5 as general review rating so I went to the cinema thinking I would see a masterpiece.Disappointing for the following reasons :scenario is similar to Interstellar but badly explained, I was lost during the movie and I did not understand what was happeningthe language learning from Rocky is ridiculous. Ryan Gosling speaks a high level English and Rocky knows only 3 words, yet they understand each other perfectlythe scenes are too fast and it doesn't slow down so you can't keep up with the events. It never slows down.So yeah, overall, I do not think it is worth 8.5. I usually rely on iMDB ratings but this one is an exception.

title: Project Primary School
review: Project Hail Mary is a solid, enjoyable film, even if it never quite reaches greatness. It doesn't try to reinvent the genre, but what it does offer feels increasingly rare: a straightforward story with genuine heart.The film works best when it leans into its simplicity. The plot isn't overly complicated or weighed down by unnecessary twists, which makes it easy to stay engaged without feeling like you're being forced to admire how "clever" it is. At its core, it's a human story, supported by warmth, humour, and a lead character who is both likeable and genuinely funny.Ryan Gosling carries the film almost entirely on his own, and does so well. He's not delivering a career-defining performance, but he brings enough charm and presence to keep things moving. The standout element, though, is Rocky, the alien. The design and puppeteering are impressively handled, and the character adds both emotional weight and originality to the film.That said, there are some issues that are hard to ignore. The biggest one is the central premise. It's never fully convincing why a primary school teacher is chosen as the key figure in such a critical scientific mission, and the film doesn't do enough to justify his level of expertise. It feels like something the source novel likely explores in more depth, but here it comes across as a stretch.There are also a few logical inconsistencies, particularly around the science and environmental conditions, that pull you out of the experience if you think about them too much.Overall, Project Hail Mary is a well-made, heartfelt film that's easy to watch and easy to like, even if it doesn't quite hold up under scrutiny. A good film, just not a great one-and certainly not the masterpiece some ratings suggest.

title: Great - for children
review: Walked in expecting something akin to The Martian. I got live-action Wall-E in space. Maybe that's on me for not researching the movie beforehand.It's just not a very good film. The whole thing falls apart quickly and goes from interesting to silly and eye-roll-inducing.However, production quality is very high, both visually and musically, and there's some decent humor - but a good movie for adults it is not.

title: Maybe not quite what you expected
A fun buddy comedy disguised as an epic sci-fi!
Not the Space Story I Expected, But One I Ended Up Liking
review: I went into Project Hail Mary expecting a serious, grounded space story, but right from the beginning, it felt like the film didn't fully take itself seriously. There's a lot of humor, quirky behavior, and a kind of "look how cool I am" energy that can feel a bit forced at times. It's not bad, but it does give the impression that the movie is trying hard to stay entertaining through constant levity rather than relying on tone or atmosphere.That said, Ryan Gosling does a great job. His performance carries a lot of the film, and his charm and quirky mannerisms create genuinely funny and engaging moments. I enjoyed watching him, even when the tone wasn't exactly what I expected.The biggest surprise for me is the direction the film takes. Most space movies treat space with a sense of seriousness and mystery, which makes sense given how vast and intimidating it is. This film, however, leans much more into personality, humor, and accessibility rather than that heavy, mysterious tone.As the story progresses, though, things improve. The tone becomes more focused, and the core relationship in the film becomes stronger and more engaging. That's where the movie really starts to work. It finds more emotional weight, and the story begins to feel more meaningful rather than just quirky.By the end, I appreciated it a lot more than I did at the start. It's still not the kind of serious space story I initially wanted, but it grows into something more heartfelt and engaging in its own way.Overall, it didn't match my expectations, but I ended up liking it.

title: Not as good as they say...
review: I went in having high expectations, but after watching it, I was slightly disappointed. The story was good but the pacing was so weird and slow in the end. It was funny. The cinematography was really good. But it just felt like a kids movie. Honestly surprised it's PG-13. Not a thriller of any type. This is a bit overrated in my opinion. Still good, but not as good as they say.

title: Meh.. or how to mess up a great story
review:
I loved, LOVED the book, and I had been looking forward to this movie for months.I was a bit dissapointed.Did not like the first half of the movie. It started off as a very lightweight, 'funny' story. It was just all superficial fluff. Too much comedy and too much music. Grace's feeling of despair and confusion never came across.Almost all the science was missing. N
No character development.Hated the karaoki scene. Totally unecessary.The ending was rushed. It had so many holes (in the story) and felt anticlimactic.My boyfriend thought the beginning was boring, but he enjoyed the second half with Rocky.My sister (who listened to the audio book) said she would have preferred a different movie as well.A mini series would have been much better to properly tell the story. And please, without the Disney / Hollywood treatment.

title: Very faithful to the book, but less hardcore science
review: I went into this after listening to about 10 out of 16 hours of the audiobook, so the story and characters were still very vivid in my mind.Overall, I did like the film. I was impressed by how much it tried to stay faithful to the source material. For a space science fiction story with so many complex ideas, that is not easy to pull off, and the effort shows.That said, I had a couple of issues.First, the casting of Ryan Gosling did not quite work for me. In the book, Ryland Grace feels more like a slightly awkward, deeply focused scientist and teacher. Gosling brings a lighter, more relaxed, sometimes even goofy energy that changes the tone of the character in a noticeable way.Second, and more importantly, the movie shifts its focus. The book is driven by scientific problem-solving, and that is where much of the tension comes from. In the film, that aspect feels reduced. Instead, the story leans much more into the emotional and artistic portrayal of Grace's relationship with Rocky.That relationship is still compelling, but the balance feels different. The sense of urgency and seriousness that runs through the book is not as strong in the movie. The film feels lighter, and at times avoids the weight of the situation.One other thing I found myself thinking about is how highly rated the film is. Going in, I expected it to stand strongly on its own. Personally, I feel that having read or heard the book made a big difference. Without that context, I am not sure I would have fully followed or appreciated several parts of the story.At the same time, I can see why many viewers still enjoyed it. The film presents its ideas in a confident and visually engaging way, and the emotional core is clear even when the science is complex. For many, that is enough to make the experience feel complete.In the end, I would say the movie works well as an adaptation, but it is a different experience. It captures the heart of the story, but not quite the depth of the science or the gravity of the stakes that made the book so memorable.

title: Good...
review: It feels brutal to say but it's a little overrated. It is a GOOD movie do not get me wrong, but not the revolutionary, heart shattering, life changing movie you'd expect from the way people talk about it. If I had just seen the trailer and checked it out i'd think wow pretty good film, but the hype online making it seem like one of the all time greats made me come out of the cinema just feeling a bit lost.

title: Do not expect the same story as in the books
Why Did I Cry Over a Rock?
review: Not a huge sci-fi person, but the hype got me. The first half left me genuinely confused. I had no idea what I was watching or where it was going, but somewhere along the way the film grew on me.The blend of humour, drama, friendship and hope hits harder than expected, and the visuals are gorgeous. And yes, I cried over a rock.

title: "Where Intelligence Meets Empathy: A Story of Becoming"
The vibes of The Martian with Ryan Gosling
review: I went to a pre-premiere screening in IMAX. Overall, the movie is good; it feels like a pretty standard film with a decent advertising budget. While watching it, it reminded me a bit of The Martian with Matt Damon (same writer), Interstellar by director Christopher Nolan and Arrival by director Denis Villeneuve. You can also spot various references to other films, such as 2001: A Space Odyssey.Let's start with the positives: it's actually not bad to watch. Ryan Gosling is a pleasure to watch. The comedy is quite funny, with some good jokes. I have no major complaints about the visuals.But to be honest, the plot and storytelling seem a bit weak to me. There are various plot holes, and the narrative feels a bit muddled. The action scenes come across as somewhat trivial. The film feels a bit drawn out.However, you can still enjoy the movie, but I wish it had turned out better. It's a normal movie you can watch for fun, but it's nothing outstanding.

title: Project Hail Mary recipe
review: Just saw this movie 3 days ago ... and I consider it a solid 7 out of 10. I never read the book, so did not know what to expect. Overall, I was slightly underwhelmed -- mostly because there was not enough originality. Instead, it seemed the movie borrowed so much from other IP's. Here's the recipe: Start with a bowl of 2001 Space Odyssey, blend in some comedy beats, then add 1 tablespoon Interstellar, 1/4 teaspoon of The Martian, a pinch of Deep Impact, and by all means don't forget 1 gallon of E. T. Blend for 35 minutes and bake for 2 hours. I felt the suspension of disbelief was so much easier to achieve with The Martian movie than with Project Hail Mary. I still enjoyed PHM - just not as much as many others.

title: READ THE BOOK
review: A very average adaptation of a genuinely excellent book. No- it is not too late to read the book! In fact now is the perfect time! You will find a completely different story that does not involve the guesswork this movie created. Ryan Gosling was the perfect person to play Ryland. The cast, the sound, costumes and effects were all top shelf. But, there were things...very important things, that were missing. So many opportunities to fill in details that would have made the movie more engaging, that were not taken. It would have been easy, but they were lost. Which makes this a lazy adaptation...read the book! You will love it!

title: Did I watch live action Wall-e?
review:
Don't get me wrong, solid 7, so worth watching.Never knew the novel existed, so cannot compare. The film is PG feel good family movie (maybe if one is 12/13 then it looks different). Nothing that we have not seen previously in some form. Good jokes, nice visuals. But let's do not make it 8/9/10, it is not a classic.RG was great (as he almost always is so no surprise)
A tad too long for one sitting.

title: Well done
review: I have not read the book, so I can't comment on fidelity. As a stand alone, this is a great movie. It's long, but I never felt that it was dragging, and there aren't any scenes I would cut. The transitions from past to present were done exceptionally well. Normally movies that do that just make me confused, but this one did not. The timing of it was just right, and the way they signalled the change by rotating the camera was perfect for me. The science is definitely fiction, but it's believable within the context of the movie. The story is great. It's human, gritty, funny, attention-grabbing, and heart-warming. The ending left me satisfied. The actors also played their parts very well. I probably wouldn't watch it again because I'm not a big science-fiction person, but it's an excellent movie, I did enjoy it, and would recommend it to others.

title: Rock solid
review: Despite the improbable story line Ryan Gosling is a safe pair of hands to carry this movie and make it entertaining.Great sound, cinematography, and special effects from industry stalwart IL&M.Being a Kiwi enjoyed the use of Po Atarau at the end of the movie; a somber, heartfelt farewell song to NZ troops departing for war.

title: Not the movie we will remember
PROJECT HAIL MARY IS ACTUALLY GOOD !
review: This film is excellent. I didn't have any particular expectations and I was absolutely captivated. I wouldn't go so far as to say it's THE film of 2026 or the best science fiction film... but it's really very good. Seeing the audience at the previous screening leave the theater with positive comments was quite amusing. The script is full of humor, but it's always a genuine and realistic humor that hits the mark. The music is excellent, as are the sound effects: that explosion, oh my days! I learned a lot, even if I didn't understand everything... The characters are engaging and the idea of the-last-chance-project is simply devastating. The film contains scenes of incredible beauty. The directing and writing is so different from other films that I forgot who the main actor was.

title: Decent, engaging sci-fi with heart, but uneven pacing.
review: Project Hail Mary is a decent film overall and definitely one that kept me engaged. The story is compelling, the writing is strong, and the cinematography is genuinely beautiful. Visually, it does a great job of pulling you into its world, and there's a real sense of care in how the film is put together.One of the strongest parts of the movie is the buddy dynamic. The chemistry there really works and gives the film a lot of its soul. That emotional connection added warmth and personality, and it helped elevate the story beyond just being another science-fiction survival tale.That said, I do think the film was longer than it needed to be. The first hour especially felt uneven in terms of pacing, and I found it harder to stay fully locked in during that stretch. I also think the movie could have done more to emphasize the stakes and tension. For a story built around such a major situation, it sometimes felt less urgent than it should have.Another issue for me was the audio during the retrieval scene, which could have been much better. That moment needed cleaner and more effective sound design to really land the way it should have.Overall, Project Hail Mary is a solid watch with strong writing, beautiful visuals, and a heartfelt central relationship, even if its pacing and tension do not fully live up to its potential.

title: Disney and Hallmark
review: This film could have very well been the result of a collaboration between Disney and Hallmark, using Interstellar as its visual blueprint. It is a heartwarming space odyssey that replaces cold science with a story about the universal power of friendship. However, Ryan Gosling's performance feels surprisingly flat, lacking the haunting depth and nuance he previously delivered in Blade Runner 2049. Despite this stale lead acting, the movie remains perfectly suited for the whole family, celebrating emotional connection and hope over technical jargon.

title: Now I know why the 6/7 meme was made
Tries too hard to be funny
Decent sci-fi flick without lasting impact
review: This movie combines dramatic elements, science fiction, adventure and comedy which makes for a good watching experience. While it touches on topics of deeper meaning (amongst others: friendship, loneliness, end of world,...), it did not make me ponder significantly.The acting is strong and the cinematography adds visual interest, but the film never quite pushes beyond being simply enjoyable, leaving little that lingers after the credits roll.

title: Touching, funny and captivating.
review: Although the way this space adventure is written and filmed keeps you enraptured with the plot and sense of adventure, it is easy to forget that Ryan Gosling spends most of his time acting alone, and he deserves much credit for his seamless performance. Apparently, no green screen was used during the filming with a set built for the spaceship interior and an animatronic or puppet used for Rocky, with SFX added to enhance it afterwards.Based on Andy Weir's second novel, it has some similarities in structure to The Martian, in that a lone Spaceman must use science and his wits to save himself, and in this case, both humanity and his newfound friend, an Alien called Rocky. What is so enjoyable about this film is not the sense of looming disaster but rather the story about how two species meeting for the first time in the vastness of space learn to communicate, work together to solve the riddle of dying stars and to eventually become friends. It is a smart mix of tension and heartfelt adventure. It is the kind of film I can say is a departure from the formulaic expectations we might have from sci-fi disaster movies. So much is so good here that, for pure entertainment value, it is difficult to fault and I already want to watch it again.

title: Every joke landed
review: Very much like many other films that came before it, when faced with ineluctable demise, Earth sends its best and brightest scientist hurtling through the cosmos in search of an answer to avoid its impending doom. Well... maybe not the best. I never read the book, but Project Hail Mary has been on my radar since the first trailer dropped.There were hints of comedic elements in the film, but what I wasn't expecting was for it to be a buddy sci-fi comedy with heartfelt moments that really stick with you.Space on its own is terrifying enough: the instant freezing temperatures if exposed, the complete absence of gravity, debris from the far corners of the galaxy, etc. These are only a few examples of the many threats one would face while exploring the final frontier. But the real challenge is the loneliness-the complete void of space where there is no up nor down. Just Dr. Ryland Grace, a middle school science teacher portrayed by Ryan Gosling, with literally the fate of the world riding on his shoulders, all by his lonesome.Until he meets another traveler, whom he names Rocky, given his distinct features.It's rare for me to see a film that hits me in the feels and demands a genuine emotional response. Watching the duo coexist, develop a form of communication, and work together to achieve a singular goal is something that works incredibly well in the film.In the infinite vacuum of space, the two lonely astronauts find each other and share experiences ranging from frustration to jokes to puppeteered figures, showcasing to audiences that the relationship between the two characters feels real-something even admirable.So yeah, get your tissues ready.There's a lot of beautiful imagery throughout the film, complemented by some well-done editing. The runtime might be a bit much for some, but in my personal experience the story is so engaging that audiences will barely notice it. All the jokes and gimmicks land beautifully, Ryan Gosling is fantastic, and though we've seen similar stories before, it somehow still feels fresh. It also knows exactly how to pull on the heartstrings of its viewers by focusing on some heavy themes that remove audiences from their seats and place them on the vessel, asking the same questions as our lead.All in all, this is a great piece of cinema that takes us on a voyage in search of answers while discovering friendship in dark and unsuspecting places.

title: Not Interstellar-level, but surprisingly emotional
review: Project Hail Mary is one of those rare sci-fi films that manages to feel clever, emotional, and genuinely entertaining at the same time. It may not reach the philosophical depth of Arrival or the cosmic grandeur of Interstellar, but it succeeds beautifully at being something warmer and more intimate: a story about survival, communication, friendship, and hope.What works best is the human core of the film. Beneath the science, the mystery, and the space setting, there is a very sincere emotional journey. The story keeps unfolding in a way that stays engaging, and the central relationship ends up becoming the true heart of the film. That is where it really shines.Ryan Gosling delivers a very likable performance, balancing intelligence, vulnerability, and humor without ever losing the emotional weight of the role. The film also deserves credit for making complicated scientific ideas feel accessible and fun without talking down to the audience.Visually, it is strong and immersive, but the real strength of Project Hail Mary is not spectacle alone. It is the sense of wonder and humanity running through the entire experience. The film knows when to be exciting, when to be funny, and when to slow down and let the emotional moments land.If there is one limitation, it is that the film does not aim for the same existential or philosophical impact as the greatest sci-fi classics. It is more heartfelt than profound. But that is not really a weakness so much as its identity.Overall, Project Hail Mary is a smart, moving, and very satisfying sci-fi adventure with real heart. A film that reminds you that science fiction works best not only when it explores the universe, but when it explores connection as well.

title: And they say good sci-fi doesn't exist anymore
review: Not if Ken and a weird rock creature have anything to say about that!Yeah I've pretty much become numb to science fiction stories nowadays, and personally I blame the Star Wars franchise for that. How are we supposed to care about plots that represent the inherent humanity of characters when they're stuck inside such generic, contrived takes on what should be fantastical worlds beyond imagination??That's where Project Hail Mary shines, because it is a deeply human story that embraces the flaws of human error, biology, loneliness and mortality that just so happens to have sci-fi elements. Perhaps it runs maybe 20 minutes too long and there are a few eye-rolling moments, but the movie compensates for that with some of the greatest cinematography I've witnessed on the big screen in a hot minute, to the point where the camera is easily its own character.Lord and Miller got to make their own Han Solo film after all. There is hope yet for the world.

title: Hollywood Debut of Academy Award Nominee Sandra HÜLLER
review: Since STAR WARS (1977) and ALIEN (1979), so many science fiction films have graced the stage, and yet there's always a story that truly captivates and moves us. PROJECT HAIL MARY is a very positive surprise. And that's primarily due to the lead actors. Academy Award nominee Ryan Gosling plays a nerdy scientist who has to travel into space to find the first true friend of his life. And Academy Award nominee Sandra Hüller, in her first major Hollywood production (from Amazon MGM Studios), is captivating as an Angela Merkel-inspired super-organizer who, at the right moment, is prepared to force others to "make their happiness happen." And then there's this: Just like in TONI ERDMANN (2016), for which the exceptional actress received her first European Film Award, Sandra Hüller delivers a remarkable singing performance, becoming another magical moment in a film that is certainly not lacking in special moments.The story: Due to a loss of solar energy, Earth is on the brink of an ice age. In just a few years, millions of people will die. A molecular biologist (Ryan Gosling), who has always held a minority opinion, works as a substitute teacher and is recruited by an ESA employee (Sandra Hüller) for the Hail Mary project. The mystery of the energy loss is indeed uncovered. Only a dangerous space mission can save humanity. And so, against his express wishes, the molecular biologist finds himself aboard a spaceship. For him, this marks the beginning of a journey into the unknown, on which he will make many astonishing discoveries.Alongside world-renowned stars Ryan Gosling and Sandra Hüller, James Ortiz plays a crucial role as Rocky in this remarkable film. It takes some getting used to him and his character. But then something happens that underscores the film's unique magic. As a viewer, you allow yourself to connect with an utterly alien being and are richly rewarded.A magnificent film that celebrates the strength and fearless intelligence of nerdy outsiders! A truly excellent film!

title: Project Touch Down!
review: Adapted from the novel by Andy Weir (author of The Martian) comes this beautifully shot Sci-Fi epic which comes full of heart and hope rather than dredd and destruction. Ryan Gosling plays the role of Grace perfectly, balancing reluctant-hero and vulnerability with humour and smarts.Visuals are great, the science seems feasible and it feels original - or at least an original take on a Hollywood space-drama.The CGI looks good, especially in the form of Gosling's 'co-star' - the newly acquainted alien "Rocky". How they've managed to create a character you care about, you laugh at, you cry at...and he has no face!? A testament to the film-making.The story flicks back and forth in time with the opening sequence already aboard the spaceship. The 'how we get to this point' element punctuates the next two and a half hours in the form of memories and flash-backs. It just works. Don't be put off by the runtime normally utilised by much more serious affairs, despite the 'global extinction' basis of the story, Hail Mary manages to steer towards the lighter side of catastrophe with much more levity than one might expect. Similar I suppose to The Martian, although it does have it's tense moments.A proper cinematic experience, go see it on a big screen.

title: Reminiscent of 1980s Sci-Fi
review: "Project Hail Mary" reminded me of that period of time during the 1980s when aliens in movies were beings to befriend rather than to fear. It also has a light-hearted, goofy, family-friendly vibe that brings it closer to "E. T." than something like "Interstellar." If you listen to the other members of my family, it's a mixed bag. I enjoyed it more than my wife and two teenage sons. I did think it was way too long, and more than once got fidgety waiting for the thing to get to the point. But I mostly enjoyed it.Ryan Gosling is perfect casting in a movie like this. He's alone on screen for much of the time, and he's so naturally likable and charismatic that you don't mind the lack of other actors. If this movie feels a bit reminiscent of "The Martian," another white dude drifting in space and trying to figure out how to survive story, it should, as both are based on novels by Andy Weir. But whereas the protagonist of "The Martian" was a frat boy who I wanted to punch in the face, Gosling creates a humbler and nerdier hero who's much easier to like.Believe it or not this is the first 2026 release I've seen, and I could have done worse.Grade: A-

title: Earnestness is a Virtue
review: It's so refreshing to see a well made sci fi movie that completely leans into its premise and trusts its audience to follow its wavelength. I don't like praising a movie for what it's not, but the total lack of cynicism is one of its biggest strengths. More importantly, it's a fully realized story with compelling characters who feel realistic and human, even though one of them is about as far from human as movie aliens get. Consider me pleasantly surprised.

title: Film first, read it later!
review: As a big fan of the excellent book this is based on, I was very excited for the film. However all those around me in the cinema who didn't know the book enjoyed it loads more than those of us who knew it's origins.It's stunning to look at, well acted, skillfully directed and as short as it can be - so certainly worth seeing in IMAX.But buy the book on the way home.Trust me ;)

title: Sometimes words as brave are difficult to explain until you fell it
review: Normally, I avoid explaining what I like in a film and end up not writing any review. I didn't even write one for Interstellar. However, I feel that this film is like therapy. Beyond being an exceptionally well-made film, Project Hail Mary has already become a part of me, to the point where I already feel nostalgic for it's end. I had forgotten how we are a fragment of humanity in the middle of this vast universe and how the wonder of life is inexplicable, and even when we think we are small enough, there is something about being "brave" that helps turn this smallness we are into something greater and that gives meaning to everything. If you have time, take a moment to join Rocky and Grace on a long journey that ultimately shows us that even in gestures that seem small to us, we can make a big difference. 8/10 - but the lesson and the nostalgia I felt are 10/10.

title: Good movie but a great book
Amaze amaze amaze 👎👎👎
review: I haven't really felt any joy for a while; life has been a bit tough lately. Watching this film changed that a little, and its soundtrack played a big part in it.That's always why I'm fascinated by cinema and video games-they have this power to make you see things differently. This film is a perfect example of that, with its story and the style of its soundtrack, which I find both hopeful and calming. I really love the original score of this film.

title: Genre-Defying Heart
review: I knew this film was about a guy in space on a 'Hail Mary' mission. That's it. So I expected a slow paced, serious space film as many of them are. What I got was a film that moves at a steady pace and has a range of emotions from laughter to sadness.It's quite long at 2.5 hours, but they reduce space fatigue for the audience by using flashbacks to fill in the story.Should I Watch it? Yes.

title: They got me emotionally attached to a rock, it is awesome.
review: Short and sweet: a cool sci-fi film with comedic elements and genuinely great storytelling, I am absolutely going to grab the book.Its been a fair while since I've seen a film where I genuinely felt attached to the characters, so this was a really nice experience. I went into it having only seen some images and hearing something about the sun. I was not expecting a mix of humour, intelligence, and depressing themes. They mix amazingly.The soundtrack is beautiful and the sound design creates great immersion.It makes sense now why Gosling was talking about filmmaker's duty to create films worth seeing, cause they nailed this.

title: A good adaption of the Andy Weir book
review: So, if you've read the book I give it an 8 out of 10. If you haven't read the book, I'd take off at least a star.It's a tough book do adapt to the screen. A lot of detail doesn't get picked up or described well in the movie that I really think adds to the plot. I think both main characters are well developed, their personalities and charm come through. The third main character is also very convincing in her role. It's entertaining and if you like sci-fi mixed with comedy, you should enjoy it. At 2 hrs 30 mins it doesn't drag at all.

title: a believably impossible story
review: This is how a story is supposed to work.I know it isn't real, yet they make me believe it anyway.It feels like it has to be experienced in IMAX, so we can fully take in everything : the visuals, the emotions, the sensation.That song, it's more than just a song.Ever since Harry Styles released Sign of the Times, it has always felt incredibly cinematic-and now it finally feels like it's found its "home".

title: This movie is a family friendly must watch
review: I loved this movie way more than I thought I would I watched it in the cinema and it was amazing on the big screen because this movies graphics are astonishing best I have EVER seen the space views and when he is floating next to planets is mind blowing.I also absolutely loved they interpretation of aliens because it was different and I liked that.This movie made me cry a few times I laughed lots because this movie is some how comedic even though it's just one guy and still I somehow did not get bored.The parts with the humans were okay but not great same with the reason for going up to space and backstory was pretty good.Overall this is a must watch it is a great family movie and deserves an 8.3 out of 10 total.

title: Kindred friendship in space.
review: You cant go wrong with a Ryan Gosling movie, throw in a Space expedition and i'm all in!This is something different, not your typical voyage into the unknown depths many light years aways, beyond the Science Fiction... This is a movie about forming a friendship, its a heart felt mission to somewhere.. Lets go!

title: A Different Kind Of Science Fiction Tale
A Buddy Space Adventure
review: When stars suddenly start dying across the universe, Earth sends a school teacher to investigate a lone surviving star. With the help of his alien counterpart, they set out to save both of their planets.This movie is an intergalactic buddy adventure. The story has layers looking into humanity, sacrifice, science, and connection. Given its setting in deep space, the visuals are impressive, with every opportunity taken to make it otherworldly. The science is sound for the most part, taking into account the laws of space and time and maintaining consistency. The two-hour and thirty-six-minute runtime is a bit long, with some parts of the movie moving at a snail's pace while others move at lightspeed. The visuals and the story make it worth a watch, especially for space and sci-fi fans.

title: A real surprise!
review: I have to say, I wasn't expecting a good movie. Sure, it had the makings, but I wasn't sold on Ryan Gosling as the lead, especially in space.I was shocked; the film was a mix between Memento and Interstellar. I must say, both are great films. It was well done; the 2.5 hours flew by! I recommend it, 8/10.

title: Makes you feel something
review: Fresh, compelling, honest drama in the slop pool of modern cinema.Family friendly: 9/10, may be intense for smaller kids, but a very clean film.Cinematography & Soundtrack: 10/10, simply excellent.Pacing: 9/10, tiptoes the edge of satisfying viewer details and pulling slow in some parts.Dynamics: 10/10, uses full spectrum of emotion from comedy to tears and effectively creates an investment in the plot.Measure to "epic" precedents like Interstellar: 8/10, while fully enjoyable in its own right, elements of science and character complexity come in just behind a full 10/10 sci-fi/end-of-world film.Overall: 8/10, thoroughly thrilling, re-watchable film. No shortcuts taken. A purely "sit back and watch" experience.This film is for the big screen in a full theater, where the depth of human achievement can be felt between collective laughs, gasps, and silence. I felt like it was critical to the experience.

title: Rocky makes the film.
review: It starts off a bit slow and seems like many other average space movies, then Rocky showed up. Watching the interaction and cooperation between the two characters is heartwarming and hilarious. It's a great and emotional movie. I am not surprised when I cry in a movie. I was surprised at how many people joined me.

title: The first blockbuster of 2026 is a good one.
review: This is the best film I've seen all year, but 2026 is still young, so I don't anticipate it'll stay that way for too long.I liked this quite a lot, for the most part. You can tell it was based on a book by the guy who wrote The Martian, but I thought the story here in Project Hail Mary was a little more interesting. It's got the "one man in space" thing going for it, but then the premise is also one with higher stakes that recall Interstellar, and there's also an added element to this that adds a good deal more humor and heart, making me think of E. T. the Extra-Terrestrial.It's technically pretty great. Most of it looks really good and it benefits from being seen on the biggest screen possible more so than most movies. At 150 minutes, I thought it was maybe a little long, but not too long. Could've ended around 130 or 140 minutes and I think I wouldn't have had any problems with the pacing.Ryan Gosling's good, too. He's almost always good. You've got a reliable writer, strong directors, good source material, and a good star. They all seemed to be on the same page and what you're left with is a good movie. It all sounds so easy, when you look at it that way...

title: Humanity needs this kind a movies
review: Project Hail Mary is the kind of movie that reminds you why you love cinema. It's been a long time since I felt such mixed emotions while watching a film-happiness, sadness, love, hope-everything is there. I went into it completely blind. I didn't watch a trailer; I only knew that Ryan Gosling was starring and that it was directed by Phil Lord and Christopher Miller, and that alone made me believe it would be something special.And it really was something special.The direction is fantastic, the visuals are beautiful, and the music is enormous-it melts perfectly into the writing and the story. Everything works together to create something warm, emotional, and incredibly human.At its heart, this is a movie about friendship, loneliness, change, evolution, bravery, and sacrifice. It's about doing good even when the situation feels impossible, and how goodness and courage can come back to you in unexpected ways.In a world that often feels dark and heavy, this film feels like a bright side. It brings genuine joy-the kind of joy movies are supposed to give you.

title: A Cinematically Beauty
review: I'd like to formally welcome back the art of cinema. This is a rare revival in an era of recycling pop culture slop made for millennials chasing their youth. Sure, PHM is a novel and an established masterpiece in audiobook performances. What makes this film great is its commitment to the string of emotions filling 2+ hours of highs, lows, and laughs in between. Coupled with the stunning visuals, exceptional audio mix and a powerful character connection, this film is a much appreciated break from the spat of Hollywood conglomerate churn. My 9.0 rating of this film is a hill I will die on.

title: Equally as Delightful as the Book
review: Go see this in the theater. This movie is so well done - I was worried about how they were going to create certain parts of it being mainly based in space, but they did a fantastic job with the graphics. I loved the quirky humor throughout the movie. The pace was not rushed, even for a longer movie with a lot to cover - there were no slow parts. Ryan Gosling was fantastic in his role as a scientist who never wanted to have the spotlight - we follow his journey as he is compelled to save the Earth.I did read the book years ago, so I don't know if anything was overlooked, but I believe it held up very well and really brought the pages to life. I think kids and adults of any age will be entertained with this movie. Highly recommend!

title: SCIENCE HAS NEVER FELT THIS WHOLESOME
review: I never thought I'd be this emotionally invested in a "floating dude" and a "walking rock," but here we are.Visually, it's stunning, and the music and score are amazing, but the heart of the story is what really got me. I went through it all. Laughing, crying, and honestly feeling so stressed for them. Sciene has never felt this wholesome!Amaze. Amaze. Amaze.

title: Great yet imperfect
review: Having loved Project Hail Mary (especially the audiobook), I went into the film with high expectations and it largely delivers. The visuals and score are absolutely stunning,I stayed through the entire credits just to take it all in. Ryan Gosling perfectly captures Ryland Grace, and Rocky is brought to life brilliantly, with some new dialogue that genuinely surprised and entertained me.That said, the adaptation does feel simplified. The depth of science and Grace's intelligence don't come across as strongly as in the book, and some key emotional beats particularly the relationship with Rocky don't hit quite as hard. The major twist also lacks the impact it had in the original story.Overall, it's a visually spectacular and very enjoyable sci-fi film, but it loses some of the depth and emotional weight that made the book so special.

title: Spectacle and character done well
review: Project Hail Mary delivers exactly what it promises and then surprises you with how much heart it has.Ryan Gosling is as charming as ever, and it's the perfect casting choice. He carries the film almost entirely on his own shoulders, and you buy it. You have to, because you're stuck with him for most of the ride. Luckily, he makes isolation feel engaging, not empty.What really elevates the movie is its balance. It has the spectacle you expect from a space film, huge ideas and beautiful scale, but it never forgets the smaller, human moments. The character work hits just as hard as the visuals.And those visuals are genuinely stunning. This could have easily looked like a standard space adventure, but instead it feels imaginative, vibrant, and alive in a way that goes beyond what you pictured reading the book.Then there's Rocky.Rocky is exactly what you hope for, lovable, weird, funny, and endlessly fascinating. The movie does choose to spoil his introduction early, which might frustrate some fans, but honestly it's the right call. He's the hook. He's the "Baby Yoda" of this movie, the thing that gets people in seats and talking afterward.Where the film stumbles a bit is the beginning. It feels rushed, like it's sprinting to get all the setup out of the way. Even knowing the story beforehand, it can feel a little disorienting. The film is clearly eager to get to the good stuff.But once it slows down, it really finds its rhythm.The core of the movie becomes Ryland Grace and Rocky, two beings from completely different worlds, united by determination, curiosity, and problem solving. Their dynamic is the heart of the film. Watching them figure things out together, the "sciencing," is fun, even if it sometimes feels compressed for time.Still, what lands is their teamwork, their persistence, and their willingness to trust each other when everything is on the line.That's what sticks.In.

title: Goofy yet beautiful
review: I felt the movie walks the line between solid science based fiction and light hearted comedy in a perfect fashion.I was incredibly surprised by the film, it is a full on space odyssey with the perfect balance of realism and fiction, drama and comedy - it's a really original story, with absolutely stunning visuals and cinematography and at its core a moving message of loneliness, friendship and overcoming life's obstacles.I feel strongly this was Ryan Gosling best performance, I always commented that his roles are very similar in each movie he makes, but some of the scenes in Hail Mary had quite the dramatic charge and Ryan really delivered!Plus the movie is mostly him so it was even more incredible how he sustained his performance consistently.It's a fantastic cinematic experience, the soundtrack was also an amazing choice and of course the practical effects and production design elevated the movie to the next level.It's silly at times but as someone already mentioned, the world needs silly these days.Go see it, it's the best movie this year so far.

title: A Review by Someone Who Hates Sci-Fi
review: I genuinely hate science fiction. Like, I would rather watch a movie about animated talking animals - any animals - than sit through a sci-fi film.So when my girlfriend offered to go see this one, I said yes for one reason and one reason only. I really wanted popcorn. And I figuredRyan Gosling would crack a few jokes and it'd be over fast.What she did not mention was that it's two and a half hours long. She does that sometimes.She's sneaky like that.And then - goddammit - I got pulled in. I actually liked Gosling in this. Which is impressive considering he spends roughly 80% of the movie completely alone. Or at least, alone as a human being.Then Rocky showed up. A rock. With arms.Rolling around in a glass bubble. I rolled my eyes when it took Gosling five whole minutes of screen time just to start talking to the thing.My girlfriend told me it takes much longer in the book, so fine. I'll let it go.But the relationship Gosling builds with this rock - this rock with arms in a bubble - actually got to me. The story was genuinely moving. And yes. There may have been emotions. There may have been moisture near my eyes. But I will deny that until my deathbed.Under oath. In front of God.And the cinematography. Jesus. I can barely imagine what this looked like on an IMAX screen.Good job. This movie surprised me enough that I'm giving it a near-perfect score.That said - I still hate science fiction. There's an exception to every rule though, right?

title: Lives up to the hype
review: Like the book, the movie lived up to the hype for me. Beautifully shot, Gosling is fabulous, and it respects the source material while making an accessible, visually stunning film. By necessity it reduces some of the character's inner monologue that the book featured, while still giving viewers an understanding of who he is. The "alien" is well done and the special effects feel grounded and realistic.

title: Project Hail Mary (2026): The Greatest Love Story of the Year Disguised by Hollywood as an Epic Sci-Fi Adventure
Grace and Rocky: A Heartwarming Journey
review: When the trailer was released, I saw how many people were already hyped for the movie because of the book. Almost everyone who had read it said the trailer was exactly as they had imagined, so I had high hopes. Plus, I loved the previous movie adapted from this author's work.In simple words, I just loved it. The relationship between Grace and Rocky was so well-done, it was heartwarming and deeply moving. I see nothing but love for this movie everywhere, and it truly deserves it.

title: Haircut
review: Despite Ryan Gosling's unrealistic self-made haircut, I really loved the film. I hope I can have a teddy Rocky. It was not perfect but there were laughings and fun. If you didn't like Interstellar, Hail Mary is a perfect compromise. But some sources say that even if you liked Interstellar, you can love Hail Mary.

title: Loved the Film, Missed the Science
review: The film was a visually stunning and heartwarming journey that I truly enjoyed, especially the bond between Grace and Rocky. However, the complex scientific depth of the novel felt rushed, leaving me craving the complex problem-solving that made the book so unique. I really wish this had been adapted into a mini series instead of a movie. A series format would have allowed the space to explore every scientific detail, giving viewers the same intellectual thrill that readers of the Andy Weir's novel experienced. It is a great cinematic experience, but "Hard Sci-Fi" deserves more time to breathe.

title: Epic story with the right amount of jokes
review: Normally i do not write reviews about movies, but this one really caught my thoughts. It is fascinating how they made this movie so interesting and captivating, while the biggest part was someone being alone in space with an alien species. It is a movie where you really get sucked into the storyline and hope everything starts to fall into its place, which also makes it sometimes very emotional. It gave me Interstellar vibes, but in a different and its own unique way.

title: An unavoidable experience
review: This movie perfectly adapts the storyline from the book and the science concepts used in the movie are also right. It was a wonderful experience to watch the movie it's miraculous with the visuals, perfectly paced storyline , and background score was also spot on. As having a curiosity for space and science i loved this movie.

title: A great movie about a reluctant genius
review: There is so much to love about this movie. The science, emotional isolation, and first contact wonder. This is a great film about cooperation and not just survival. Intelligence, empathy, and curiosity can overcome fear, nationalism, and extinction-level stakes.We only just finished the 2026 Oscars and Project Hail Mary arrives as an early contender for next years Academy Awards. And a great year for Space too right before the Artemis mission. But more than the space backdrop was the emotional connection to the characters, the dialogue that is quick and keeps pushing the movie forward, the acting, music, and special effects makes this movie instantly a favorite.

title: To save the planet!!
review:
Watching Project Hail Mary was a really enjoyable experience for me. As someone who loved the book by Andy Weir, I went in hoping it would capture the same feeling and overall I think it did.Fans of the novel will probably feel relieved seeing how much of the heart of the story made it onto the screen while people who havent read the book will likely just be amazed by the story itself. Of course some things had to be cut especially parts of the deeper science but that's expected when adapting a book like this into a film. Even with those changes it still feels like a strong and respectful adaptation.A big reason the movie worked so well for me was seeing Ryan Gosling in the lead role lol cause Some of my favorite movies already feature him, like La La Land and Blade Runner 2049, so seeing him here immediately made the experience even better. He has a way of bringing a lot of personality and emotion to his characters and that really helped make the story feel more alive.His performance made the movie more engaging and honestly elevated the whole experience for
me.Overall, I just really enjoyed the film. It captures a lot of the same emotions that readers felt while going through the book-the wonder, the tension, and even the quieter moments of discovery. Even though it's not a perfect one-to-one translation of the novel, it still delivers the same spirit and feeling that made the story special in the first place.

title: A Visual Spectacle! Great Characters, Heartwarming Theme, Roller Coaster Of Emotions
review: Project Hail Mary is a visual spectacle with great characters, a heartwarming theme and a roller coaster of emotions.Science teacher Ryland Grace (Ryan Gosling) wakes up alone on a spaceship light-years away from earth only to learn that he must save the Earth from a mysterious threat that is killing the sun. He makes an unlikely friendship that might be the key.This film is based on the book by the same name by Andy Weir. I cannot speak to whether this film is accurate to the book or not but the film definitely works on its own. Project Hail Mary truly had me engaged for its full run time. It's full of suspense, humor and fascinating science. If you're concerned about seeing this film because it's too long, I guarantee you it's not long enough. Ryland Grace is a character who I could relate to, mainly because of his journey in this film. Initially, he is in denial of his purpose but eventually he comes to terms with what he has to do, which is extremely important. Rocky (James Ortiz) is the surprise highlight of this film. I was blown away at how much I could feel and care for a rock and Rocky really takes the film to another level. The mix of puppeteering and CGI flows seamlessly for his character. However, it's not just the actors' performances that make this film great - it's also having the film take place in space and the fact that we only see two characters for the majority of the film. Those things made me feel as if I was right there with them. When you add the beautiful cinematography that this film has, it exceeds overwhelmingly. I love that this film can be seen by anyone; it's such a well-written, feel-good movie, which is hard to find nowadays. This film hits it out of the park!The film's message is about companionship and how people and living creatures need someone there with us so we are not alone. The way this theme is conveyed in the film is very heartwarming and touching.I give Project Hail Mary 4.5 out of 5 stars and recommend it for ages 8 to 18, plus adults. The film releases in cinemas on March 20, 2026. Be sure to look for it.By Rayce S., KIDS FIRST!

title: I.N.C.R.E.D.I.B.L.E
review: Holy cow! This was a top notch movie! I have never read the book but I thoroughly enjoyed this amazing movie! Rocky is an amazing character, but the communication between Rocky and Grace is a little bit unrealistic, but who cares! I also feel that there were moments in this movie that could have been cut because they seemed to just make the movie feel longer. I would highly recommend this movie, it was absolute cinema!

title: A Story of Friendship
review: Unlike other sci-fi works that approach saving the world with heavy drama and dense emotions, this film maintains a light tone and effortless humor. It is a story about friendship, clearly designed to win the audience's hearts, and it succeeds brilliantly. I truly cared about Grace and Rocky. In fact, making Rocky so charismatic despite being a faceless "rock" is a massive achievement, alongside Ryan Gosling delivering his signature charm in yet another great performance. It is an excellent, optimistic film that never feels over-the-top. I loved it.

title: Top-Notch All the Way
review: Absolutely met expectations. Ryan Gosling is one of the most entertaining talents on the big screen these days. He has a penchant for finding the humor beneath the drama and always delivering that without undermining the story or depth underneath.I've loved Gosling since his Half Nelson and Lars and the Real Girl days and his career has taken the kind of turn you expect from someone with as much charisma as he has. What I appreciate is that, while he's committing to much larger scale projects and doing less character work than before, he's continuing to make great choices within that sphere in terms of where he lends his talents.This film is incredible all the way around. The visuals are spectacular and interesting despite the limited locations. Miller/Lord do wonderful work staging what I'm sure was a very difficult set to stage. Performances are top-notch. Most importantly, as an audience member, I laughed a ton, I cried, I sat on the edge of my seat...there wasn't a moment I became disengaged.Go see this in theatres if you still can. It's absolutely worth it.

title: One man. One impossible mission. One unlikely friendship that could save humanity.
review: So finally caught Project Hail Mary on theatre, and damn... it hit different. Since I've never read Andy Weir's original novel, that actually made my theater experience even better. No constant mental comparisons or "they changed that!" moments. I went in completely fresh knowing only the basic premise from the trailers. A regular guy wakes up alone on a spaceship with amnesia, facing an impossible mission to save Earth. And wow, it delivered pure, brain-tingling joy.Ryan Gosling absolutely owns the role of Ryland Grace. He plays this reluctant middle-school science teacher turned interstellar savior with the perfect mix of sarcasm, panic, quiet brilliance, and growing determination. He never feels like a generic hero. Grace is flawed, funny, and deeply human, and Gosling makes you invest in him from minute one. Sandra Hüller delivers one of the standout supporting performances as Eva Stratt. She brings a fierce, no-nonsense intensity to the role of the driven project leader who recruits Grace and pushes the entire Hail Mary mission forward.The film is smart without ever feeling like a lecture. Directors Phil Lord and Christopher Miller keep the energy lively. There are some signature witty beats and sharp editing that prevent it from getting too heavy, even during the more introspective parts.Visually, it's built for the big screen. Those IMAX shots of deep space, the sun in peril, and the ship's interiors make everything feel immense yet intimate. The practical + CGI blend for certain elements feels grounded and believable instead of over-the-top flashy. At about 2 hours 36 minutes, it moves well overall. There's a slight mid-section breather where Earth flashbacks fill in context, but it never drags enough to lose you.One of the best and most satisfying sci-fi movies in years. Smart, funny, visually spectacular, and emotionally rewarding. A pure gem with real emotional punches.

title: A Much-Needed Dose of Cosmic Kindness
Sublime.
review: I rarely write reviews on this site. But as this is an adaptation of one of my favorite novels of recent times, this was necessary.Being a Big fan of the novel (reading the book 3 times), and one of my favorite novels of all time, What can I say? A masterpiece of adaptation. With a talented screenwriter like Drew Goddard adapting the work and talented directors like Lord and Miller, I already imagined it would be at least great.Ryan Gosling was perfect in the role. This film will be nominated for several awards, it will be nominated for several Oscars, and Gosling will probably be nominated for best actor.Ther score of Daniel Pemberton (Spiderverse 1 2 3) is sublime, like a Vangelis score type, Greig Fraser (Dune parts 1 2) Cinematography it´s top notch, Charles Wood Design (from many MCU films) it´s out of this World. The effects are perfect, but for a certain character most of them (for the happyness of me and many fans ) they are made of practical effects and I always thought that was the right way to create them. Who read or watched the movie knows who I am talking about.Of course like in every adaptation, some things are removed, like some characters and scenes, but the main plot of course is here, and is faithful.This is a great year for sci-fi fans. Project Hail Mary adaptation, Disclosure Day from Spielberg (his return to directing aliens), Dune Part 3 from Villeneuve.I feel that Project Hail Mary will be one of the films with the most Oscar nominations in 2027, along with Christopher Nolan's Odyssey. But I hope it's time for the first alien and space film to win best picture, director, and screenplay.

title: AMAZE. About as good of an adaptation that you could ask for.
review: The book is my favorite of all time, and this movie is top 15 for me after my first watch. I cried, I laughed and I smiled. Ryan Gosling and our new friend were perfect.The visuals were astoundingly good (no surprise though, Greg Frasier), the score was beautiful, and the sets were amazing. You can tell this was a passion project for many.There were certain things about the book that I wish were still in the movie, like more in depth science, but I understood why they had to leave them out or quickly address it. Overall I was very satisfied with how they went about the adaptation.

title: An Epic Yet Intimate, moving and enduring Sci-Fi Triumph
review: Project Hail Mary feels like an instant modern sci-fi classic. It's both epic and intimate-and that contrast is exactly what makes it so special.On one hand, it's epic in scope and ideas, delivering a visually stunning and thrilling story with a powerful message about cooperation. No matter how high the stakes, it reminds us that connection, empathy, and care for one another are what truly matter.On the other hand, it remains deeply intimate. Much of the film focuses on the relationship between two unforgettable characters, watching them learn, connect, and bond despite being completely different in every possible way-all while trying to save each other's worlds. That emotional core is what elevates the film beyond its premise.The science is impressive and will satisfy any sci-fi fan. While the premise of the film may not be entirely new, it feels fresh and original in execution. The atmosphere is outstanding, thanks to the cinematography, score, and remarkable visual effects.The script is exceptional, delivering a complete and satisfying arc for its characters. Yes, the film is long-but that length allows every moment to build toward a payoff that feels earned and deeply rewarding.The characters are the true highlight. Ryan Gosling delivers one of his most endearing performances, supported brilliantly by Sandra Hüller and James Ortiz. And of course, the direction by Phil Lord and Christopher Miller shines throughout-it truly feels like their film, blending science, humor, heart, and high-stakes storytelling into a seamless whole.Overall, this is a moving sci-fi epic that absolutely earns its place as a modern classic.

title: One of the best sci-fi films of the decade.
A masterpiece blend of Arrival, Interstellar, and The Martian
review: I saw a pre-release showing in Dolby Cinema. It was incredible. While it had to streamline a lot of the science in the book, this is as good of an adaptation as one could possibly hope for. The breathtaking cinematography by Greig Fraser and fantastic performance by Ryan Gosling cement it as one of the sci-fi greats.

title: Top tier heartfelt sci-fi
review: Project Hail Mary is a prime example of a genuinely crowd pleasing blockbuster that has everything. It's a really funny and deeply heartfelt sci-fi adventure with an impressive ability to ground a high stakes journey across the stars in a more down to earth friendship which ensures the film is incredibly investing until the very end. It's a wonderful celebration of what the human race can achieve when everyone works together and how bravery comes from a selfless need to protect others.So much rests solely on Ryan Gosling's shoulders here and his phenomenal leading performance quickly rises to the task whilst offering a welcome reminder of how naturally lovable he can be. His comedic timing is flawless and balanced by plenty of emotional moments when needed, allowing him to keep proceedings fairly lighthearted without disregarding the world ending stakes. He only improves when he meets Rocky, who is adorable and their bond is entirely believable.The direction by Phil Lord & Christopher Miller is never anything less than astonishingly good as they take the necessary time to make you care about these characters so the climactic and technically faultless spectacle is especially tense. Greig Fraser's cinematography is equally accomplished throughout and the decision to transfer the digital version over to film gives it a lovely texture alongside some masterfully handled aspect ratio changes for long periods, making it a must see in IMAX.The general lack of green screens is definitely felt in the best ways since the film always looks amazing and every scene in outer space becomes a technical marvel. The visual effects used to bring Rocky to life are the strongest because he feels tangible for all of his screen time. As well as having impactful moments of silence, Daniel Pemberton's transcendent score pairs beautifully with any of the most heartwrenching and visually stunning sequences for maximum effect.

title: "Whoomp! (There It Is)"
review:
I was honestly really surprised by how good this film is and how much fun I had with it.This isn't just a "good sci-fi film" it's one that pulls you in right away and doesn't let go.The sound, the visuals, the CGI it all feels not just high quality, but thought-through. Nothing feels random. You can tell there was real effort behind every decision to make it look and feel exactly like this and that's exactly why it works so well on the big screen.QuestionRyan Gosling carries the film.You believe everything. The scientist trying to figure things out. The guy who's sometimes just overwhelmed and that mix is what makes it work.The jokes land because they don't feel like jokes. They just happen and that's what makes everything feel real.QuestionAnd the film still has weight to it.You always feel what's at stake without the film constantly reminding you. It's just there the whole time.QuestionSandra Hüller also surprised me in a good way.You can tell she comes from a different background, but that actually gives her role something unique. She fits in without trying too hard and yeah the karaoke moment sticks.QuestionAnd then Rocky.No big explanation needed:
he just works.He grows on you, and the chemistry between him and Gosling takes the film to another level."Fist my bump."Light, almost funny.And then:"You sleep. I watch."And you immediately feel what's behind it. That dynamic between them is what makes it so strong.QuestionI also liked how the film switches between past and present.How everything comes together piece by piece without ever feeling complicated.
